detailed_eligibility: To be considered for the UG Tata Scholarship at Cornell University, an applicant must fulfill the eligibility criteria given below:Nationality:Must an Indian citizen/resident.Be a pass out from any Indian senior secondary school.Financial Requirement:Must qualify for need-based financial aid.English Language Proficiency:Must submit eitherTOEFLor IELTS score.TOEFL-iBT:100IELTS:7.0Admission Status:Must have an admission offer fromCornell University for any undergraduate degree program.Prospective applicants must note that special preference will be given to students from art, engineering, agriculture, applied economics and management, biological science, life science, and other science students. | Application: The students are supposed to complete the College Scholarship Service (CSS) profile to fulfill the requirements of the application process. As a part of the application process, students must completetwo application forms before January 2, 2024:Cornell University Tata Scholarship ApplicationNeed-based Financial Aid Form for International StudentsSome important details to be provided at the time of the application are:Self-income and parent’s incomeNoncustodial parent asset and incomeCitizenship documentsPhotocopy of the Passport | Selection: Students need to fill the details in the application form carefully because the Cornell University Tata Scholarships is awarded purely based on the details and the documents that have been submitted during the application process. Some of the aspects that will be looked into for the selection are:Academic qualificationsFamily incomeCitizenshipEnglish Language Proficiency test ScoreThe shortlisted candidate list will be released on the official portal of Cornell University and hence the students after applying are suggested to have a consistent look at the portal for the results and any other important notices. Recipients of the scholarship will be funded for the duration of their Cornell undergraduate program.

The eligibility criteria may differ based on scholarship type and the university it is offered with. Suitable candidates are nominated inthe winter sessionfor getting admitted to thefollowing fall, spring or winter terms.To be eligible for receiving OTS, an applicant must:be aninternational studentwith a validCanadian student visa.be proficient in English by the time of application.intend to enroll in adoctoral-level, full-time degree-granting graduate program.Applicants Must Current not Hold other Canadian Scholarships including but not limited to the Queen Elizabeth II Graduate Scholarship in Science and Technology (QEII-GSST), UW Provost Graduate Scholarship.Must be willing to enroll in a doctoral program at a university in Ontario.Have achieved a minimum average of 8.0 on 10.0 scale in the full-time master’s degree which is equivalent to 80% in Ontario.The candidates not have completed a ug or pg degree at any university or college in Ontario immediately after applying for admission to the doctoral program under the OTS.not be a current student at a post-secondary institution in Ontario at the graduate or undergraduate level. | Application: Each eligible university’s unit of graduate studies oversees the application and selection processes for the Ontario Trillium Scholarship. Every institution has its own application process for the OTS program.Following are the documents required to apply for the Ontario Trillium Scholarship:Appropriately filled application formA copy of all the university transcriptsA valid Canadian study permitA copy of the applicant’s average calculationProof ofstandardized tests like IELTS, SAT,etc.Candidate’s letters of recommendationA list of the applicant’s research contributionsA list of awards and scholarships received by the applicantApplicant’s CVCandidate’s plan of studiesThe OTS letter of support from an authorized supervisorProof of good healthMost universities like theUniversity of Toronto,University of Ottawa,York University, etc. automatically nominate eligible high-performing students who have applied for admission. | Selection: A selection committee, chosen by the Vice-Provost, Graduate and Postdoctoral Studies (OGPS), is charged with evaluation of the files and selection of the recipients of the Ontario Trillium Scholarship. Students are selected on the basis of their merit and program criteria.Terms and ConditionsA candidate can request for an interruption of a Trillium by getting approval for up to a year’s leave. The interruption of a Trillium cannot exceed12 consecutive monthsand can be approved in case of:Maternity or parental leaveImmigration processCompassionate leave (example: bereavement)Medical or family leaveA recipient will have to repay the award received if he/she:Transfers to another institutionCompletes the program prior to the scholarship’s natural endWithdraws from the programCould not maintain acceptable academic standingLoses full-time statusLoses eligibility for the renewal of OTS

The Ontario Graduate Scholarship Program is jointly governed by the Ontario government and the participatingOntario universities. The deadline to apply for the scholarship is established by the Province but varies according to the university.CitizenshipBoth domestic and international students are eligible for the Ontario Graduate Scholarships. As per the guidelines, the applicant must be:A Canadian citizen or permanent resident at the time of the application;An international student studying in an Ontario university with a validCanadian study permit.Note: Students protected under subsection 95(2) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act(Canada)are also eligible to apply for this scholarship.Academic RequirementsTo be eligible for the Ontario Graduate Scholarship, applicants must fulfill the following academic requirements:Hold anA– Average (Ontario equivalent of 80%)in both the years of full-time study before being awarded the scholarship.International applicants must be proficient in English by the time of application.Applicant must be registered full-time in a graduate degree program, that is,60% or more of a full course load as defined by the university40% or more for students with disabilities.Eligibility Conditions for Ontario Graduate ScholarshipsMaster’s students are eligible to receive the scholarship for a maximum of 2 years. Whereas, doctoral students may receive the scholarship for 4 years.The lifetime maximum restriction for a government-funded scholarship is six years for any student.No single student can avail two government-funded scholarships concurrently. The restricted scholarships are listed below:Queen Elizabeth II Graduate Scholarship in Science and Technology (QEII-GSST) (formerly OGSST)Ontario Trillium Scholarships (OTS)Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (SSHRC)Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council (NSERC)Canadian Institute of Health Research (CIHR)Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships (Vanier CGS) | Application: The OGS program adjudication takes place at the program level, therefore deadlines and specific processes are determined by an individualmaster-level program at Canadian universities. However, while applying for the scholarship, students have to submit the following documents:Ontario Graduate Scholarship 2024 application formConfidential Academic Assessment Reports from two academic referees.Academic transcripts from previously attended post-secondary institutions.Copy of photocopy of the proof Canadian citizenship/ permanent resident status or copies of visa documentation.Plan of study (maximum of 3,950 characters including spaces)Previous awards/publications/research experienceUniversities Offering Ontario Graduate ScholarshipsThe universities offering OGS program are tabulated below:Brock UniversityWilfrid Laurier UniversityYork UniversityTrent UniversityCarleton UniversityUniversity of TorontoWestern UniversityUniversity of Ontario Institute of TechnologyQueen’s UniversityUniversity of WindsorLaurentian UniversityUniversity of WaterlooUniversity of OttawaMcMaster UniversityRyerson UniversityLakehead UniversityUniversity of GuelphNipissing UniversityStart date & payment scheduleAward Start DateRecommended Payment Activation Form Submission Deadline to Department1st Payment2nd Payment3rd Payment3-session Award Period2-session award PeriodSummer (May)Two weeks after SGS offer letterLate July at the earliestSeptemberJanuaryMay 1 to April 30May 1 to Dec 31Fall (September)August 1SeptemberJanuaryMaySept 1 to Aug 31Sept 1 to April 30Winter (January)December 1JanuaryMaySeptemberJan 1 to Dec 31Jan 1 to Aug 31 | Selection: There arethree areas of evaluationunder which each OGS 2026 application will be evaluated. Respective program departments and faculties determine the weight placed on each of the following criteria:OGS Selection Criteria for Masters level:CriteriaDescriptionWeightingAcademic ExcellenceTranscripts; Other academic achievements; Type of program; Course load; Duration of course; Prior scholarships and awards30%Research Potential and Statement of InterestStatement of Interest (motivation, creativity, communication skills); Relevance of work experience; Significance and merit of proposed research; Initiative and contribution to research and development30%Personal Characteristics/ Academic Assessment from RefereesWork experience; Leadership experience; Involvement in academic life; Project management20%OGS Selection Criteria forDoctoral level:CriteriaDescriptionWeightingAcademic ExcellenceAcademic records; Scholarships and awards received; Duration of the previous course30%Research PotentialPlan of study; Judgment and originality; Initiative and autonomy; Interaction with supervisors & peers; Ability to lead and complete projects within the allotted time.50%Personal Characteristics/ Academic Assessment from RefereesQuality of presentation; Publications; Professional relevant extracurricular interaction and mentoring20%Conditions for

To apply for Australia Awards Scholarship, applicants must fulfill the following eligibility requirements:Citizens and residents of a participating country like India, Bangladesh.Not a permanent resident of Australia or New Zealand.Overs 18 years of age on February 1 of the scholarship’s commencement.Proficiency in the English language, with a recent score of IELTS or TOEFL exam.IELTS: 6.5TOEFL-iBT: 78PTE: 58Satisfying the entry requirements of the chosen Australian university.Fulfilling the Australia visa requirements of the Department of Home Affairs.Candidates mustnothave previously been a recipient of Australia Awards unless they have lived outside Australia for double the time period of staying in Australia. | Application: To apply for the Australian Awards Scholarship, students must complete the following steps:Step 1:Choose home country and award type to check whether you are eligible for the award.Step 2:Once eligibility is established, review the course eligibility criteria.Step 3:Register and complete the online application form for Australian Awards.Documents RequiredCertified copies of following original documents along with their English translations (if required)graduation degree certificateFormal academic transcriptsBirth certificateCitizenship document’s certified copy (such as national identity card or passport).Resume concisely detailing work history, responsibilities and research experience.At least one academic referee report and one other referee report is required.Ph.D. or research-based master’s degree applicants must provide two academic referee reports.Copy of English language test (TOEFL, PTE orIELTS) result.The certificate must be valid till January 1 of the scholarship’s commencement year.For PhD candidates: Evidence (e-mail or letter communication) that a potential supervisor has granted in-principle support for the research topic. | Selection: All applications submitted for the Australia Awards Scholarship are considered on merit where every applicant is given equal opportunities. The scholarship selection process will be administered by Program Areas in various countries or regions. Therefore, the process varies according to the region/country.Factors affecting Australia Awards ScholarshipsAcademic competencepersonal and professional qualitiescapability to influence the home country’s development challengesBased on these factors and many others, each participating country produces a list of shortlisted candidates. The number of students depends on the Australia Awards Scholarships available to the region. Shortlisted students are interviewed and the selection process may include other activities like group participation sessions to measure the suitability of applicants.After that, each participating country produces a list of candidates, with their first preference course and institution. Placement requests are reviewed by theAustralian universitiesand based on the merit, they process a placement offer to applicants fulfilling the admission eligibility criteria. This includes obtaining anAustralian student visaas well.Australia Awards Scholarship decisions are announced/informed byAugust 1.

To be eligible for this fellowship program, candidates must meet the admission requirements of the Stanford MBA program.Bachelor’s degree(in any field that is equivalent to a U.S. bachelor’s degree)SubmitGRE/ GMAT and IELTS/ PTE/ TOEFLscores. The minimum score requirements for the same are:TOEFL-iBT: 100IELTS: 7.0PTE: 68Submit letters of recommendation and statement of purposeOptional questions (200 words)Work experience (optional)ResumeApart from the above requirements, the students will also be evaluated based on the leadership role they have taken, and professional work experience. The student must demonstrate how they will contribute to the learning community of Stanford both on campus and off campus. | Application: To be considered for the Stanford Reliance Dhirubhai Fellowship Program, applicants are required to complete the scholarship form along with the Stanford MBA application form. Following documents will be required from students seeking fellowship for pursuing Stanford MBA:Mark sheets showing educational performance in class 10th, 12th, and graduationEssay of 250-Words (on a given topic)Resume or Curriculum Vitae(CV) [Should be of one-page]Awards, Certificates, and Recognition (both academic and social)Employment history (years of experience, name of the organization, and type of work)GMAT/ GRE Official ScoresFinancial and tax-related documentsFamily income (annual)Official transcriptsLetter of recommendation: Applicants are supposed to submit two LORs highlighting their professional careers.Note:This fellowshippre-applicationform does not require any fees. | Selection: A total of 50 candidates are shortlisted during the evaluation process for the fellowship program. The candidates are selected based on their academic standings, financial need and overall application profile. However the acceptance rate for scholarships is only 5.4% which makes it highly competitive to earn. Among the 50 shortlisted candidates, only 3 are selected for fellowship based on their leadership qualities, skills, work experience (if any), financial requirements and overall profile.The Standard Reliance Dhirubhai Fellowship program only considers those candidates who demonstrate strong academic standings and completed their senior secondary education in India.

The candidate must be an Indian citizen, residingin India for at least six monthsat the time of application. In addition to this, all suitable candidates for Inlaks Shivdasani Foundation Scholarships must fulfill the following requirements:No candidate should be over the age of 30 yearsMust be an Indian passport holderMust not hold a postgraduate degree from abroadCandidates who are currently studying a postgraduate degree abroad are not eligible for this fellowshipEvidence of admission is mandatory before applying for fellowshipHold a first-class undergraduate degree from arecognized university in IndiaInternational university graduates must have stayed in India for two years before applying.Have fulfilled all the admission requirements of the chosen coursework.For Social Sciences, Humanities, Law, Fine Arts, Architecture and related subjects, applicants must have a minimum academic grade of 65%, CGPA 6.8/10, or GPA 2.6/4 from a recognized university/institution.For Mathematics, Sciences, Environment and related subjects, applicants must have a minimum academic grade of 70%, CGPA 7.2/10, or GPA 3/4 from a recognised university/institution.Also, the applicant must NOT hold a master’s degree from an international university. The applicant must also not have already begun a postgraduate degree course abroad at the time of application.Fellowship Specific Eligibility RequirementsEach fellowship has some specific eligibility criteria. The following section discusses the criteria of all the fellowships individually:Inlaks Fine Arts AwardsCompleted graduation or post-graduation from any esteemed Indian institution.Final year visual arts students are also eligible.If you don’t have a formal degree in visual arts, you must have prior experience in hosting art exhibitions.Inlaks Fellowship for Indian Classical MusicMust have some formal training in Indian classical music.Inlaks Theatre AwardsThe candidate must be below the age of 35.Completed graduate or postgraduate degree from any recognized Indian University.Candidates who do not have a formal degree will be considered based on their notable past achievements.Inlaks Research Travel GrantThe candidate must be below the age of 35.Must have either a first-class in Masters degree or a first-class in bachelors (honors) degree.Those who have attained a first-class in both graduation and postgraduation will be given preference.Must have a confirmed Ph.D. program registration in Humanities and Social Science at any recognized Indian institute for a minimum period of two years but not exceeding four years.Apart from the above-mentioned scholarships, the Foundations also gives internships, fellowships, grants, and awards under Inlaks Ravi Sankaran Programme, AAA Artist Grant, Film, and Television Awards, Sports Awards, etc. | Application: Things to keep to keep in mind while applying for scholarship:Submit the online application for scholarshipMust hold a valid Indian passportOnly one submission per candidateThe applications that do not meet the minimum admission and financial requirement will not be considered for scholarship.It is mandatory to submit at least 2 letters of recommendation with one from the academic counselor or teacher.Documents Required for ApplicationThe following documents must be submitted along with the application form:Evidence of admission to a course or a universityFinancial requirements of the institute, written in detailPhotocopy of the first and last pages of passportAt least onereference letter- this can also be emailed directly to the institute atapplications@inlaksfoundation.org.The subject line of the email should be as follows: Reference Letter | Name of Applicant | Email Address of Applicant.Candidates applying in the field of architecture, fine and applied arts, film, dance, acting, and music, must upload their portfolio as a PDF file or share an online link.Candidates are to verify all the information before submitting the application. In case an error persists, candidates must email the same with a screenshot atapplications@inlaksfoundation.org. | Selection: The selection of Inlaks Shivdasani Foundation Scholarship awardees is carried out by an independent Inlaks Selection Committee of the Foundation, who analyzes and evaluates all the applications, and calls promising candidates for the interviews. Applicants are not only evaluated based on their past and present performances but also on their potential as demonstrated in the application. The applicants applying to arts and design courses will be evaluated on the basis of their portfolios.The Foundation conducts scholarship interviews either in Delhi or Mumbai in May or June.

Requirements for the Rotary Foundation Global Grants are quite straightforward and specific and are based on the applicant’s project.An eligible applicant’s project must:Have a long-term and sustainable impact on the societyAddress an essential area identified by the community.Includes activities that align with the foundations’ beliefsHave a minimum expenditure budget of 30,000 USDIn addition to these, candidates must meet the following academic requirements:Minimum academic qualification and performanceLanguage proficiency (the language of the study country or instruction)Strong public speaking and interpersonal communication skillsHigh ethical standards demonstrated in personal, academic, or professional lives | Application: Along with the application for the Rotary Foundation Scholarship- Global Grants, individual applications by the members of the vocational training team and scholars must also be submitted.Applicants must provide proof of admission in a university’s graduate-level program or a letter of invitation for conducting postgraduate-level research in their applications.Applicants must intend to receive education in a foreign country.Applications regarding travel for vocational training teams, scholars and volunteer travelers must be submitted 90 days before the travel dates. | Selection: The Rotary Foundation Global Grants have their own panel of the selection committee:.Grant decisions are based on the application forms and the further interview sessions of the shortlisted candidates.Interview can take place in-person or via live video conference, which will be specified by the committee at the start of the process. The date and time are also confirmed then.Rotary Foundation consists of over 35,000 clubs that work together to address the world’s most challenging issues. It has members in almost every country and has headquarters in Evanston, Illinois, USA. It also has associate foundations in Brazil, Australia, Canada, India, Germany, United Kingdom, and Japan.

Applicants for Hornby Scholarships must fulfill the following eligibility criteria:Have three-year full-time English Language teaching experience and a UG degreeUG degree should be equivalent to a bachelor’s degree in the United Kingdom.Have a minimum of5 yearsof experience as an English language teacher or trainer.Must be involved in some developmental work as a trainer or manager.Must be a citizen of the country from which they apply.Score well in theIELTS, TOEFL, orPTEexaminations.Minimum of6.5 GPAis required for the English language examinations.Must have supervised in the course of the English language.Note:The candidates who have applied for the English Language course in the United Kingdom for a master's degree program or diploma program are eligible to obtain financial support. | Application: Applicants are required to submit the personal, academic, professional, referees details along with a supporting statement. The details required are mentioned below:Personal details:Full nameE-mail addressDate of birthGenderNationalityResidential addressContact informationAcademic details:Name of the institutionUndergraduate degree and specialization course10+12 mark sheetCertificatesAwardsInformation about the courses which were related to English mediumProfessional details:Name of organizationPosition in the companyType of workDates when the applicant was employedJoining dateResignation letterReferees:Details of at least two referees who can give the candidate’s reference, if shortlisted for the interviewReferee’s nameProfessional informationEmail addressReferee’s professional relation to the candidateSupport statement:The candidate is supposed to justify why he or she is applying for the Hornby Scholarship in about 1,500 words. In addition, the applicant has to answer several questions like:Describe the current English teaching duties and the training that one has delivered?Is the applicant a teacher or trainee?Why does the applicant wish to study for amaster's degree at the University of Warwick?What professional skills and experience would the applicant bring to the studies?If the applicant is awarded the Hornby scholarship, in what way does it benefit him/her?How would the applicant share their learning and knowledge with English language teachers after returning to their home country from the United Kingdom? | Selection: The scholarship features a four-stage selection process listed below:Online application form –The candidates submit the online application form for an initial assessment.Interview –The applicants who clear stage 1 are called for an interview round at the office of the British Council in their country of origin.Application to the University –If the applicant is selected in the interview, he or she will be invited to apply to the university for further consideration.University acceptance –The scholarship award is confirmed for the candidate and arrangements are made for him or her to arrive in the United Kingdom to join the classes.This is a significant process that needs to be completed after the submission of the application form.

Both International students as well as German students studying at Germany’s public or state-recognized university are eligible for the funding under Deutschland Stipendium.Studying or commencing studies at UG or PG-level degree any German institution.Part-time students and prospective students waiting for admission are also considered eligible.Proficiency certificate in English or German language.For more details students may refer to The Scholarship Program Ordinance (StipV) and the Scholarship Program Act (StipG). | Application: For details regarding the application process, deadline of application, admission requirement and supporting documents aspiring students must directly get in touch with the university they are enrolled in or in which they are seeking admission.First- and higher-semester students can apply directly to their university if they provide Deutschland Stipendium.Master’s program applicants need to include the certificate from their undergraduate degree.For matriculated students, they need to include a current transcript indicating their unweighted average grade (or evidence of their average grade/final grade on the last examination or degree completed).The application for Deutschland Stipendium or German Scholarship can be submitted online via the Deutschlandstipendium official website.Note:All supporting documents must be submitted in English or German. Documents issued in other languages need to be provided as both original copy and officially certificated translation. The application period may vary from university to university, hence it is advised to check the deadlines before applying for the scholarship. | Selection: An applicant’s entire personality is taken into account during the selection process. From academic excellence to personal achievements, everything is equally considered. As per majority oftop institutions in Germany, selections for Deutschland Stipendium are based on the following factors:Scholastic and professional recognitionsInvolvement in extra-curricular activitiesSpecial achievements, if anyVolunteer work, if anySelecting the successful candidates and approaching potential sponsors with financial assistance from the Federal Government - the entire process is conducted by the respective universities.Germany recognizes and appreciates the role played by young professionals in its development, so the government aims to promote education by providing scholarships and grants. More than 7,500 sponsors contributed to raising around 29 million euros along with the government last year. Students who are recipients of the Germany scholarship are allowed to have other sources of income as well, like the BAfoG.

With over 100 scholarship programs available for international students, the eligibility criteria for each program can widely vary. However, applicants for DAAD Scholarships must meet the following essential requirements:Language Skills: Proficiency in English with a minimumIELTS band scoreof 6.5. While knowledge of the German language is not mandatory, it is highly recommended.Academic Grades: A minimum of 8.5 CGPA or 85% (or equivalent, depending on the degree being pursued) is required.No Objection Certificate (NOC): Applicants for exchange or short-term research programs must obtain a NOC from their home institution.Work Experience: A minimum of 2 years of work experience, which can include research, teaching, or graduate assistantship roles.Research Proposal/SOP: Submission of a 500-1000 word research proposal orStatement of Purpose (SOP) for Germany. In some cases, a CV may suffice.Note:Applicants must also provide proof of accommodation for visa insurance purposes as part of the application process. | Application: The DAAD application process for most scholarship programs takes place in 2 steps:Complete the online application formand upload the documents.Send a copy of the application formto the DAAD office via post.STEP IBeginning with the online application process, it is simple, direct, andtakes about 30 minutes.Applications are to be made via theDAAD Portal, whichopens around 6 weeks before the scholarship application deadline(s).Once you have completed the application form, upload the following supplementary documents:Online Application FormCurriculum VitaeNo Objection Certificate filled in and stamped by the previous institutionInvitation Letter of German SupervisorUniversity Degree & CertificatesNote:The first and last names in the application form should be identical to the name in the applicant’s passport.STEP IIAfter submitting the form and uploading the supporting documents, candidates will be directed to another page, where they can verify their application package and download a PDF file. Mail the following documents to your nearest DAAD office:One copy of theApplication Summary,which is generated in the portal (PDF)Recent reference from a previous university teacherIndian students must post their application package for DAAD scholarships to the regional address mentioned below:Regional Office New Delhic/o DLTA Complex, R.K. Khanna Stadium, 1 Africa Avenue110029 New DelhiTelephone: +91 (11) 6646-5500Fax: +91 (11) 6646-5555E-mail: info.newdelhi@daad.de | Selection: Each DAAD scholarship has its selection committee responsible for thoroughly evaluating applications. These committees may be based locally, for instance,in India for Indian applicants, orGermany,depending on the specific scholarship program. The selection process primarily considers the following factors:Project QualityAcademic PerformanceDuration of Research/FieldworkResearch Proposal/Work ExperienceAlthough formal interviews are not conducted, some programs may require you to provide a personal introduction. This will depend on the scholarship type and your location.

To be eligible for the Felix Scholarships, underprivileged applicants must opt for a full-time postgraduate program with any one of the partner universities. Also, scholarship applicants must be below the age of 30 years.In addition, forIndianscholarship, an applicant has to fulfill the following eligibility criteria:Must be an Indian nationalMust not be currently residing or studying outside India.Must have a first-class UG degree from arecognized Indian university.Fornon-Indianscholarship, an applicant must fulfill the following eligibility criteria:Must hold a first-class UG degree from a university of the applicant’s home country.Must not be currently residing outside the home country.Felix Scholarship Terms and ConditionsApplicants who already hold a master’s degree are not eligible for the Felix Scholarships.Applicants must return to work in their home country after the completion of studies. | Application: Prospective applicants should note that Felix Scholarship applications can be made to only one of the participating universities theUniversity of Oxford, School of Oriental and African Studies, and University of Reading). The application process for Felix Scholarships varies at each partner university.Application Process at the University of OxfordApplicants are not required to submit a separate application form. All they have to do is tick a box on their Oxford PG application form. The university officials use the details inserted by the applicant in necessary sections of the admission application.Application Process at the University of ReadingTo apply for the scholarship, the applicant must obtain the admission offer to the PG program at theUniversity of Reading. Upon receiving the offer, students need to complete the scholarship form at the me@reading applicant portal.Application Process at the School of Oriental and African StudiesApplicants are first required to apply for a Master’s or Research degree program atSOAS. After that, the students must complete the Felix Scholarship online application form. | Selection: Each university shortlists candidates on the basis of their academic excellence and financial need. The shortlisting process will take place in earlyMarch 2026.Shortlisted students have to complete ashort questionnaireand appear for aninterviewin India. The interview or questionnaire not only tests the academic abilities of the applicants, but also assesses their personality, financial need, and suitability for the Felix Scholarships.Candidates will be sent a letter setting out the date and venue of their interview. Each interview lasts around 30 minutes and will be held either in New Delhi or Bangalore. The decisions will be announced inMay 2026.Note:There is no interview for non-Indian students.

To apply for the GET scholarship, you need to fulfill the following eligibility criteria:Age:Applicants should be under the age of 30 years at the time of applicationCitizenship:You need to be a resident of India with a valid passportMinimum Qualification:Should have completed graduation from a recognized Indian UniversityThe applicant should have an unexceptional academic record and extra-curricular achievementsAdmission Evidence:A proof of enrollment or an unconditional admission letter for any PG course from any of the recognized UK universities beginning in the fall semester (September/October) | Application: The application process of Goa Education Trust Scholarships is quite easy and straightforward. It is a simple four-step procedure that ensures that you have successfully submitted all the necessary documents before the set deadline.For your reference, thefour-step Goa Education Trust scholarship application processis given below:Step 1:Download the scholarship application from the official website of the British Council.Step 2:Complete the downloaded form in theword formatand insert a scanned copy of a recent passport-size photograph in the relevant section.Step 3:You are required to provide proof of certain aspects such as age, address, etc. Therefore, you have to gather the following documents:Copy of birth certificate to confirm your ageCopy of passport identification page to confirm your addressCopy of conditional/unconditional admission letter of aUK university.One academic and one professional reference letterStep 4:Once you have completed all the three steps listed above, you have to mail the entire application package (application form, supporting documents, photographs) to the British Council.You can mail your application package either via email or by post. Those who want to send an email must ensure that their scanned application package is not exceeding 10 MB.Email must be sent toget.scholarship@britishcouncil.org.The ones who are unable to send their application package via email can send the hardcopy at the followingaddress:FOA: Goa Education Trust Scholarship 2021 ApplicationBritish Council Division,British Deputy High Commission,901, Tower 1, One Indiabulls Centre,Elphinstone Road (West),Mumbai – 400 013,IndiaIt is important to note that any application submitted without supplementary document(s) will be automatically rejected. Moreover, you are requested not to send any additional reference letters or supplementary documents. | Selection: Post application deadline, the selection panel from the British Council of India reviews each application on the basis of:Academic qualificationReferencesExtra-curricular achievementsOnce the application evaluation process is complete, a list of shortlisted students is compiled. Thereafter, if you are amongst the shortlisted students, you will be called for an interview session. Recipients are selected based on their interview and overall profile.All students must note that even though the scholarship scheme is open for all Indian students, preference is still given to Goan students.

scholarship_name: Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam International Postgraduate Scholarships 2020
detailed_eligibility: To be eligible for Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam International Postgraduate Scholarship, an applicant must:Be an international studentPossess an admission offer for a Masters’s level program in theFaculty of Engineering at the University of Sydney.Have a minimum WAM (Weighted Average Marks) of 75 in undergraduate courseworkTerms and ConditionsApplicants must not hold any other primary scholarship offered by the University of Sydney.Students transferring from other PG programs or those who have already commenced their course are not eligible for this scholarship.The recipients must commence their degree in the same semester and year the scholarship is awarded. | Application: Since the applications for Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam International Postgraduate Scholarships 2020 are currently open, applicants can access theonline application formon the university’s website.Along with the application form, all the applicants must upload their academic transcripts of previously attended institutions.Applicants must note that the deadline for Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam International Postgraduate Scholarship 2020 application is January 12, 2020. | Selection: Academic achievements of applicants are the main criteria for awarding the Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam International Postgraduate Scholarship.The selection committee headed by the Associate Dean (Education) will select students for the scholarship program. After receiving the online applications, the committee will evaluate the applicants and based on their academic performance, compile a list of shortlisted candidates.Shortlisted applicants will be interviewed by the committee. Selected students will be notified through an official letter.Terms and ConditionsTo renew the scholarship, recipients must maintain WAM of 65 in each of the semesters.This scholarship offers only 50% of the total tuition fees. All other expenses are required to be borne by the student.Termination of Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam International Postgraduate ScholarshipThis International Postgraduate Scholarship will be terminated if:The recipient withdraws from an eligible course or fails to enroll.The student status changes from international to domestic.The recipient does not resume study at the end of a period of approved leave.The student does not maintain satisfactory academic performance.The recipient is determined by the University to be guilty of serious misconduct.

The scholarship program is designed for an academically exceptional student at the Aga Khan Academy. As a prospective candidate, you have to complete the online Aga Khan Application and submit it at theUniversity of VictoriaScholarship website, along with all the documents.How to Apply for Aga Khan Academy Renewable Scholarship 2024?The step-by-step procedure for Aga Khan Scholarship 2024 are given below:Step I:Create a NetLink ID and sign onto your UVic My Page using the same.Step II:Select “Student Services”. If you have not yet received admission confirmation, select “ Applicant Services”.Step III:Select “View and Apply for awards/scholarships”Step IV:On my page account, select the "Aga Khan Academy Scholarship Application" and 2024-25 as the study period.Step V:Complete the application form.Step VI:Send in the required student supporting documents. Also, ask your nominator to send in the required Nominator Documentation: Form B, reference letterStudent Supporting DocumentationAwards/ Recognition Statementlisting all your awards, prizes, and recognition of the last three years. Also, how that experience can help you transition to student life at the University of VictoriaActivities statementwhich includes a list of all extracurricular and volunteer activities of the last 3 years, with proper date and hours of involvementA community reference letterwhich is written by a person( other than a family member) including details of the name, contact email, and period in which they know you, properly attested should be sent to entrance@uvic.ca.Financial Need Assessment:For this, you need to complete theBudget Sheet. | Selection: After the submission of the application form, candidates are selected by a panel of selectors. To be eligible for the Ms. Agatha Harrison Memorial Fellowship, each applicant needs to establish eligibility on several grounds.Academic QualificationsConsistent academic performanceMinimum 60% in post-graduationPh.D. degree with a specialisation in Economics, History, or Political Science.Apart from the academic qualifications listed above, the candidate must also fulfil the following requirements:Citizenship:Be an Indian national currently residing in India.Age:Must be within the age group of 30-40 years.Experience:Minimum of 3 years of teaching experience at the UG or PG level.Note:If the applicant has studied abroad for more than six months, it is then mandatory for them to live in India for at least three consecutive years. Only after fulfilling this condition, an applicant applies for the Ms. Agatha Harrison Memorial Fellowship. Moreover, they must mention the specific dates indicating the period of their stay. | Application: Ms. Agatha Harrison Memorial Fellowship applications are accepted through an online process. As a prospective applicant, they have to register at the officialMHRD website.Documents Required for Ms. Agatha Harrison Memorial FellowshipAlong with the Ms. Agatha Harrision Memorial Fellowship 2025 applications, the following documents must also be uploaded:Self-attested degrees/ certificates of all previous examinations. The records must include the DOB.In the case of foreign degrees and diplomas, proof of equivalence should be furnished (Certified by the Competent Authority).Experience letter/certificate of EmploymentResearch Proposal:Precise program of study/research (at least 500 words) stating the justification of their research/study in the U.K.Photograph & Signature:Upload a recent passport-size photograph and signature. The dimensions and size of each should be 200x230 pixels and 530 KB, respectively.After the successful submission of the application form, along with the stated conditions, the names of the candidates for Interviews will be published on the website of the MHRD Ministry, and a confirmation mail will be sent to the shortlisted candidates. | Selection: Merely fulfillment of the Ms. Agatha Harrison Memorial Fellowship eligibility and completion of the application does not guarantee selection. The MHRD selection committee scrutinizes each application based on which a list is compiled, shortlisting the selected candidates.These candidates are sent an invitation letter for an individual interview session. After the interview, the committee announces the selected name of the candidate for Ms. Agatha Harrison Memorial Fellowship. This name is displayed on the MHRD Ministry website. The decision of the committee is final and cannot be challenged.

This scholarship is specially designed for Indian Students whostudy in Canada on a student visaand is given only to those students who have superior academic achievement and excellent high school grades.Must be an Indian nationalMust have completed high school education from an Indian institution.Must have an unconditional admission letter for a UG program at the University of Alberta.Must be commencing the chosen program in the same session and year as the scholarship.Must have less than 24 IB, AP, or A-Level credits, before joining the university.Eligibility for UG Admissions at the University of AlbertaTo secureadmission in UG program at the University of Alberta, an Indian student must fulfill the following requirements:Completion of five academic subjects in class X and XII.High school academic transcriptsEnglish proficiency scoreNote:The number of scholarship offered each year are only limited and transfer students are not eligible under this category of scholarship. | Application: The University of Alberta doesNOThave any separate application form for India First Year Excellence Scholarship.Indian students admitted to aUG program at the University of Albertawill be automatically considered for the scholarship program.Prospective students seeking financial aid through this scholarship program or any UAlberta Scholarship are advised to submit their admission applications as early as possible. | Selection: The University of Alberta’s scholarship selection committee will receive applications of the eligible students from the admission office.Selections for India First Year Excellence Scholarships are majorly based on an applicant’s high school standing. To be eligible for the HASS- Excellence Scholarships, applicants have to opt for a full-time graduate program at the University of Queensland’s Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences.In addition to this, an applicant must:be anIndian citizenwho is residing in India at the time of the application.have completed anundergraduate degree from an Indian institution.have received an unconditional admission offer for a postgraduate program at UQ.The academic session must be beginning in September 2020.not hold any other scholarships simultaneouslyThose who have already received financial aid must highlight it in their scholarship application.All scholarship applicants are required to submit necessary documents to back up their claims, in order to be eligible for the scholarship.Applicants can contact the association regarding any queries at the followingemail id:engagement@hass.uq.edu.au | Application: Applicants who fulfill the eligibility criteria for the UQ HASS Scholarship for Excellence can apply for the scholarship.The University of Queensland has a separate online application process for the scholarship. However, before applying for the scholarship, applicants must receive their admission letter from the university.Admission Checklist for Postgraduate Program at UQEnglish language proficiencyTOEFL-iBT: 87TOEFL-PBT: 570IELTS: 6.7Pearson Academic: 64Relevant bachelor’s degree with a minimum GPA of 4.50 on a 7.0 scale.Standardized testlike GMAT, GRE (if applicable)An updated CVNote:Applicants must adhere to the HASS Scholarship for Excellence application deadline established by the university. | Selection: All the applications are reviewed by the selection panel and since this is a merit-based scholarship, the selection process for the HASS scholarship is based on the following factors:Academic qualificationsGRE or GMAT scoresStatement of PurposeReferencesThe selection is done solely on the basis of applications. The selection committee takes the final decision and informs the awardees via email.Terms and ConditionsStudents have to fulfill all requirements associated with their student visasOverseas Student Health Cover is a compulsory requirement of your student visa.Students are expected to perform well and achieve good results academically.Students have to participate in a photoshoot and Testimonial for the Faculty, which is published on the website and used for other promotional purposes.In the case of deferring, transfer, or withdrawal from the program, students have to inform the Executive dean in writing.The scholarship is terminated if the student does not complete the academic year at UQ or fails to maintain full-time study

The Australian National University follows strict eligibility criteria for ANU Study Canberra India Scholarship. As per the guidelines, eligible candidates will be the ones who fulfill all the requirements mentioned below:Holds Indian citizenshipCompleted of the most recent degree/certificate/diploma from an Indian institution.Enrolled in a single/doubleUG or PG program at Australian National University.Consistent academic results in secondary or undergraduate level.Received an unconditional offeradmission to the ANUbefore the deadline of the scholarship.Note:A spouse or a dependent, of an employee of diplomatic or consular missions, may apply for this scholarship at the graduate level. However, such applicants are not permitted to apply for this scholarship at the undergraduate level. | Application: Eligible candidates have to apply for the ANU Study Canberra India Scholarship separately from their admission applications.The scholarship application process is entirely electronic and must be completed by the closing dates set by the Division of Student Recruitment and published on the ANU website. | Selection: A selection committee comprises of the Student Recruitment (or nominee), the Regional Manager or Manager, International Recruitment, the Manager International Admissions, and a representative from Study Canberra. It is chaired by the Associate Director.Selection for the ANU Study Canberra India Scholarship is intensely competitive and only the very best candidates are considered for the award. It should include a title of the scholarship scheme the applicant is applying for in the subject of the email and also the PAC application number.Note:Within 10 days of scholarship application submission, applicants will get e-receipt confirming the same | Selection: Recipients of the scholarship are selected based on theiracademic meritandpersonal statement.Each scholarship application is also reviewed to ensure that the student has applied for theIreland student VISA.Note:NUI Galway reserves the right to terminate the scholarship if the recipient fails to adhere to the terms and conditions.

Based on this, the university officials select the recipient.Terms and ConditionsScholarship aspirants and recipients should adhere to the terms and conditions mentioned below:Students should enroll and begin their classes according to the Offer letter.Should meet the university’s academic progression policy.Scholarship applies to only on-campus study and does not apply to theMaster of Management course.Shouldnot be a recipientof an external government-sponsored scholarship program.Australian or New Zealand students who hold permanent residency in Australia arenot eligible for this scholarship.In case the recipient withdraw from the program before completion, scholarship moneymust be repaid.

Foremostley, to be eligible for the Sussex India Scholarship, applicants must be enrolled in an eligiblePG program at the University of Sussex.Students will be shortlisted for the scholarship on the basis of the eligibility criteria as defined by the university.Theeligibility criteriaestablished by the University of Sussex is mentioned below:Nationality:Indian or permanently domiciled in India.Proof of residency is a must.Financial Sufficiency:Should be a self-financing student and should be able to show financial support equal to the cost of attendance.The student should haveaccepted an offer for a Masters’s courseat the University of Sussex,starting september 2026There are a few factors due to which the candidates areconsidered ineligible for the Sussex India Scholarship, have a look:Students who have the benefit of any other Sussex scholarship like the Chancellors International Scholarship.Students going to pursue any of the mentioned courses will not be considered eligible for the scholarship:Postgraduate diplomasCorruption, Law and Governance LLMMA(Development Studies, Globalization, Early years in Education, Business and Development, Development and Public Policy, Governance, Poverty and Development, Power, Participation and Social Change)PGCE course and a Masters’s degree in Social Work.Students with an offer to study in the University of Sussex Business School will be able to combine this award with the Business School International Masters Award (2026). If the scholarship information is not showing on your CAS or you want to register for the scholarship after the CAS has been created, please contact Admissions:pg.applicants@sussex.ac.uk. | Selection: After the submission of the Sussex India Scholarship application form, the scholarship evaluation panel reviews each application comprehensively.While the applicant’s academic records and financial need will play a crucial role, other factors are considered at the time of selection.
