To be eligible for Deakin Vice-chancellor's Meritorious 100% Scholarship applicants must fulfill all the requirements given below:Citizenship:Only Indian nationals who are residing in India at the time of the application.Scholarship applications must be sent via authorized Deakin agent present in India.Academic requirements tosecure admission at Deakin University.Academic Eligibility CriteriaIt is important for each applicant to meet the following academic requirements established for the Deakin Vice-chancellor's Meritorious Scholarship:For UG, an overall score of 85% is required in class 12 CBSE/ ICSE / State Board; OR10 overall in the General Certificate of Secondary Education exam.A score of 32 in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme is required.For PG, an overall score of 80% in UG degree is required.Apart from these requirements, there is a minimum English language capability for every course. The applicant must fulfill that depending on what course they choose. | Application: The application process of Deakin Vice-chancellor's Meritorious Scholarship is quite flexible. Students may or may not choose to submit a separate scholarship application form.Eligible applicants will be automatically considered for the scholarship based on their Deakin University admission application form. those who choose to submit an application must do so at least one month prior to the commencement of the academic session.However, all applicants need to provide some supporting documents. Assessment of these documents will highly factor in the selection process.Student Supporting DocumentationThe supporting documents are required for the assessment of Deakin Vice-chancellor’s Meritorious 100% Scholarship 2020 are:A statement of purpose or personal statement, approximately 300 words.At least two references or letters of recommendation.Deakin admission application along with the academic transcripts.Thepersonal statementmust be written by the applicant only and should reflect the applicant’s personal views. Overall, the statement must includeDetails of any previous work experience or extracurricular activity.Brief description of why the applicant wants tostudy in Australiaand specifically Deakin UniversityThe reason why the applicant can become an outstanding ambassador for Deakin University. | Selection: Upon receiving all applications and supporting documents, theDeakin Universityofficials will start evaluating each scholarship applicant. Students will be informed about the outcome of the selection process in due time.As per the requirements, shortlisted applicants may be asked to attend an interview as a part of the selection process. In that case, the final decision will be based on the applicant’s interview session.

scholarship_name: Oxford and Cambridge Society of India (OCSI) Scholarships
detailed_eligibility: In the first place, students need to opt for any part-time or full-timeprograms at Cambridge University, Oxford University or in any of the colleges affiliated by the two to be eligible for the scholarship. Further shortlisting of the candidates is done on the basis of the eligibility criteria defined by the universities.Eligibility criteriaas per the universities::Must be an Indian citizen, residing in the country.Must not be older than 30 years of age, as of September 1, on the year as per the year of application for the award.Must have secured admission to Cambridge or Oxford University.Must have proof of residence at Oxford or Cambridge before the scholarship funds are released.Note:Candidates are supposed to get admitted in the Oxford or Cambridge University via the normal academic procedure. OCSI will not assist them in the admissions.Terms and ConditionsRelatives of the members of the executive committee of the OCSI are not eligible to apply.Students who have already received a commitment for full outside support should not apply.Students who get awarded any other scholarship support after getting the OCSI Scholarship should inform the society so that the award may be passed on to the next ranked candidate. | Application: Students applying for the OCSI scholarship should possess an offer letter from Oxford or Cambridge University or any of the affiliated colleges. Students going to apply for the scholarship are supposed to consider a few points, have a look:Application forms for the scholarship can be availed from the official website of Oxford and Cambridge Society of India by mid-February, every year.Completed applications must reach the society by May 31, 2024.All the shortlisted candidates will be required to appear for an interview at New Delhi, around early June 2024.Candidates who will qualify for the interview round will be awarded scholarships by mid-July 2024.Details required for the OCSI Scholarship programPersonal information and contact details of the applicantDetails of the university and program one opted for.Academic background of the applicant.Essay type questions related to work experience, community activities, life experiences, motivational aspects of life, future plans.Applicants can visit the official website of theOxford and Cambridge Society of India (OCSI)for any further details. | Selection: OCSI considers the overall performance of a candidate before deciding the result and awarding the scholarship. It is a highly coveted award since the number of scholarships is limited and there are a huge number of applicants.The major factors which catalyze theshortlisting of the candidatesare:Academic AchievementsCo-curricular AchievementsCommunity / Social WorkResponses Given in the Application FormNote:Shortlisted candidates are required to physically appear for an interview during the selection process.

Even though the scholarship program is available to both domestic and international students, applicants must provide proof of residency. In addition to that, students must fulfill the eligibility requirements listed below:Any overseas applicant for either a full-time taught postgraduate course delivered on campus or Master's by research delivered on campusYou must have achieved a minimum of an upper second-class Honours degree (or international equivalent grades - see below), or a CPE with a merit profileApplicants for the Graduate Diploma in Law (CPE) and Legal Practice Course (LPC) are eligible for this award.Exclusion:If you are applying for a Postgraduate Diploma, distance learning course or certain courses delivered by the Faculty of Health & Social Sciences, you will not be eligible for this awardYou can receive this scholarship alongside a Sports or Music scholarship, but cannot receive any other academic award.Part-time, student-driven and distance learning (i.e online courses) are not eligible | Application: No separate form is required for Bournemouth Academic Excellence Scholarships. Students will be automatically considered based on their admission application.They must know the required documents needed at the time of admission so that the scholarship process becomes easy for them.BU Admission Checklist for International StudentsAcademic transcripts from previous institutionsAccompanied by certified English translations.Letters of recommendationAcademic reference letter for UGProfessional and academic references for PGStatement of purposeResume (if applicable)English Language ProficiencyIELTS: 6.0; a minimum of 5.5 in every component.To be considered foradmission at Bournemouth University, applicants must provide the documents listed above. Once the applicant is offered admission, the application will be sent for the scholarship evaluation. | Selection: Applications of the eligible candidates will be sent to the selection committee. They will then evaluate the admission application. Accordingly, students will be shortlisted and scholarship will be awarded.Factors Affecting the Scholarship Selection DecisionThe recipients of the Bournemouth Academic Excellence Scholarships are selected based on factors like :Academic ExcellenceFinancial requirementUnconditional admission offer from the universityProficiency in the English languageSelected students will be contacted by the university through an official email.

For programs commencing in 2026/27, scholarships are only given to high achieving applicants in the form of a reduction in tuition fees. Apart from this, prospective candidates must fulfill the given requirements for being eligible for the University of Lincoln India Scholarships:Must be a citizen and current resident of India.Must be a first-year student at the PG course at University of Lincoln.conditional/unconditional offer from the university2:2 Bachelor’s degree from a recognized university (minimum grade 50% overall)As an alternative to IELTS, we accept a minimum of 70% in the Standard XII awarded by CISCE or CBSE (for courses requiring IELTS 6.0).Must be proficient in the English language:ELP Test NameMinimum Score RequirementIELTS6.0 overall (no section below 5.5)TOEFL-iBT79 overallPTE Academic58 overall (no section below 51)University of Lincoln PEASS coursePass at 40% or aboveNote:The English language requirements of the prospective candidates should be submitted along with completing the application form atintscholarships@lincoln.ac.uk. | Application: University of Lincoln India Scholarship does not require any separate application form, eligible students will be automatically considered for the award. Only those students who secure conditional or unconditionaladmission to University of Lincolnwill be considered for the scholarship award. | Selection: Selection CriteriaIn order to be eligible to receive the India Scholarship, prospective students must meet the following criteria:Be a national of, or have permanent residence in, India.Hold a Conditional or Unconditional Offer from the University of Lincoln for a full-time postgraduate taught programme commencing in September/October 2025 or January/February 2026.Have been awarded, or be expected to receive, one of the following qualifications:- A Bachelor's degree from a recognised institution with a minimum grade of 2:2 or equivalent.- A Pre-Masters qualification from a recognised institution with a grade comparable to a 2:2.- A Master’s degree from a recognised institution with a merit grade or equivalent.Please visityour country webpagefor more detail regarding regional entry requirements that are equivalent to a 2:2.

Indian students must have completed their undergraduate degree from a recognized institution, that is equivalent to a US bachelor's degree such as:four-year bachelor's degree; orcompleted master's degree, orfull-time postgraduate diploma ( if UG degree is of less than four years' duration)Applicants who fulfill this basic requirement must verify the eligibility criteria mentioned below:Academic Qualification:Minimum 55% or higher in an undergraduate degreeSkills:Leadership skills and community service experience.Professional Experience:Minimum of 3-years full-time (paid) experience in the related field of study.Language Proficiency:Minimum acceptableIELTSscore of 6.0 or equivalent TOEFL/PTE score.For the language test waiver, students must contact the USIEF.Eligible Fields of StudyEach applicant should have enrolled in one of the fields of study:EconomicsEnvironmental Science/StudiesHigher Education AdministrationInternational Legal StudiesJournalism and Mass CommunicationPublic HealthSustainable Development/SustainabilityUrban and Regional PlanningNote:Each applicant should choose one field of study that matches his/her chief area of interest. The individual program may have a different structure and curriculum depending upon the academic host institution due to subspecialties, course offerings, and academic requirements. | Application: Fulbright-Nehru Master's Fellowships applications are accepted through an online process. As a prospective candidate, you have to complete the online Application form availablehere.The step by step procedure to fill Fulbright Nehru Master’s fellowship is given below:Step 1:Visit the web page and create an account.Step 2:Provide personal details and click "Continue".Step 3:Applicants will receive a pin by email which they would need to enter and proceed.Step 4:Create a password and log in to the account.Step 5:Provide personal, contact, and academic information.Step 6:Provide information on language skills, study plan, personal statement, university preference, etc.Step 7:Upload the required documents.Step 8:Fill in the grant and additional information and submit the form.Review the entire application before submitting it. The application should be free of grammatical and spelling errors.Documents RequiredApplicants are required to submit the below-mentioned documents along with their online applications:Academic transcripts (scanned) from previous institutions, accompanied by certified English translations.Statement of purposeUpdated CV/ResumeAcademic degree and mark sheetsExperience CertificateEnglish Language Proficiency Test ScorecardMinimum 3Letters of recommendations(Professional and academic references for PG)Copy of passportNote:Applicants must go through theInstruction formand keep documents ready beforehand. This will help them to submit the form easily. Students should also be aware ofNehru Master’s Fellowship terms and conditions. | Selection: The candidate did not need to apply separately to U.S. institutions, this isdone by IE/Fulbright Program, which will send their applications to four different institutions.The awardees for theFulbright Scholarshipare selected based on their overall performance in academics, work/experience skills, competency, skills and experience in community service. The selection committee alsoreviews the CV and essay submitted by the student,at the time of application.After a careful examination of all these, the candidates are shortlisted and are informed by the USIEF thereafter. Timeline and Placement Process for the Fellowship:May 14, 2025Application DeadlineEnd June 2025Review of applicationsEarly August 2025USIEF informs the applicants about the review outcomeEnd August 2025Interviews of short-listed candidatesEnd August 2025USIEF notifies principal and alternate nominees. Nominees take tests (such as TOEFL and GRE)October 2025USIEF forwards applications of recommended candidates to the U.S. for J. William Fulbright Foreign Scholarship Board (FFSB) approval and placementMarch/April 2026USIEF notifies the finalists about the FFSB approvalMay/June 2026Pre-Departure OrientationJuly/August 2026Pre-academic training in the U.S. (if required)August/September 2026Degree program beginsPlacement of shortlisted recipients is done by the Institute of International Education (IIE), New York. The recommended candidates are not required to separately apply to US institutions as the IIE/Fulbright program will apply on their behalf to 4 different institutions. While doing so, the preference of the candidates will be taken into consideration.Note:Those candidates that have previously applied to the U.S. Institutes must notify USIEF immediately.

scholarship_name: Lady Meherbai D Tata Education Trust Scholarships
detailed_eligibility: To be eligible for Lady Meherbai D Tata Education Trust Scholarships 2025, you must fulfill the followings criteria:Indian women graduate from a recognizedIndian universityShould have a consistently remarkable academic universityApplied for admission to reputed accrediteduniversities/institutions in the UK, Europe, or the USAMinimum of two years of work experienceStudents applying or studying for part-time, short-term, or correspondence courses or have already connected their studies abroad are not eligible to apply for Lady Meherbai D Tata scholarships.NOTE: Fresh applications for 2025-2026 is now closed. | Application: If you are eligible for Lady Meherbai D Tata Education Trust Scholarships 2025, send an email atigpedulmdtet@tatatrust.org, requesting for the scholarship application form. The email should strictly include the documents mentioned below:Course you wish to pursueThe university you are planning to attendCourse FeesSource of Funding (in brief)Current profileBased on the eligibility criteria and supporting documents, the Trust will email the application form. You have to send the filled-in forms along with required documents to the Trust Office at:Mulla House, 3rd floorHomi Mody StreetMumbai 400 001Lady Meherbai D Tata Education Trust Scholarship application form 2024-25 should be completed very carefully and sent before the prescribed deadline. | Selection: After the evaluation of the applications, the Trust shortlists students for apersonal interview with the Board of Trustees. Information regarding the date and time of the interview will be conveyed to each student via email.Since the Trust’s headquarter is based in Mumbai, all the interviews will be held there itself. The travel expenses for the interview have to be incurred by the students only.A gift scholarship amounting up to 8365 USD per student is awarded to students selected after the personal interview. However, selected students must show proof of at least half funding to cover their tuition fee andstudent housing in the USAor UK.

In order to be eligible for this scholarship, students are required to fulfill the eligibility criteria mentioned below :Students who possess Indian Citizenship are eligible for this scholarship.Students are required to demonstrate financial needs and outstanding academic merit.Students must have completed their class 12th grade from CBSE or any other recognized boards.They are required to demonstrate English proficiency in terms ofTOEFLorIELTSscores.An IELTS score of 6.0 or a TOEFL score of 90 is required. | Application: Students will be considered automatically for the scholarship when they apply for the courses for which this scholarship is applicable. No separate application is needed.Applications forms can be downloaded from the official website of the university. Students are required to fill the application form and send it through email or courier to the address provided below.Applications should have been submitted before thedeadline i.e. March 1, 2023Students are required to download the pdf of the form available on the website and fill it.Students can submit their application form by either email or courier.Students are required to submit all academic transcripts and other relevant documents demonstrating financial need and academic merit.The supporting documents such as medical documentation are required to be submitted asseparate pdfs.The duly filled application form along with required documents can be mailed toGEAsubmission@dal.ca.The supporting documents such as medical documentation are required to be submitted asseparate pdfs.Alternatively, students can also submit the form through courier to the below-mentioned address:Registrar's Office (Awards) sHenry Hicks Academic Admin Bldg - Rm 130. | Selection: International students are eligible for various scholarships and grants at the University of Dalhousie. They are required to submit theGeneral Entrance Award Applicationirrespective of their nationality.The submission makes students eligible for various scholarships automatically. Indian Students enrolled in theBachelor of Commerce programandBachelor of commercein market management will only be considered for CorpFinance International Limited (Kevin Andrews) India Bursary.They are required to submit documents listed below for the selection:Students are required to submit the transcript of 12th grade.They can also submit certificates of activities that help them to demonstrate their academic excellence.They are required to demonstrate their financial needs.On the basis of the academic merit and the financial need, students will be shortlisted for further rounds of evaluations.Students will be called for Interviews at the university on the basis of no. of scholarships available.The final list of selected candidates will be published on the basis of the required criteria specified along with the number of scholarships available.

To be eligible for the Skoll Scholarship, applicants must either be a social entrepreneur or be a part of an impact career. An impact career professional is someone who has used entrepreneurial approaches to address the same social or environmental issue. They can demonstrate they have a clear core thread that unites their work throughout their careers.These professionals can demonstrate a commitment to social change in a particular area through this thread of work. To get the benefits of the Skoll Scholarship, the applicant must have spent at least 3 years in his or her social venture, meaning that the applicant has:Started a social ventureTackling a social or environmental issueCreated an impact initiative inside an organizationThe applicant would also have to explain the need for a financial grant like the Skoll Scholarship as well as demonstrate why business education is fundamental in an entrepreneur’s case. | Application: To get the benefits of the Skoll Scholarship, Students are supposed to apply forMBA at Oxford University.Applicants will be required to write a scholarship essay at the time of submitting their MBA application.After being selected, the second part of the scholarship involves logistics and reference form including basic short questions like how the applicant got to know about the opportunity or the references.A written application is followed by a virtual interview and further a personal interview foradmissions at Oxford University. Also, there is no age restriction for applying to the Skoll Scholarship. | Selection: Candidates will receive an update on the status of their scholarship application and whether they have been shortlisted shortly after admissions decisions have been made. Final shortlisting is planned to take place in late March with candidates expected to be invited to interview in April and May. The candidates are also required to take GRE or GMAT. Candidates have to take IELTS or TOEFL as the English Language proficiency tests.Applicants who are unsalaried or have an income which may exhaust if studying forMasters of Business Administration (MBA) in the UKor paying the admissions fee can apply for a fee waiver from the center. The applicants can send in their CV and cover letter atskollscholarship@sbs.ox.ac.uk. Applicants are required to apply for the fee waiver before submitting the MBA application.

As mentioned earlier, the National Overseas Scholarship is valid at variousuniversities in Canada, Germany, US, etc. Along with fulfilling the admission requirements of the chosen university, applicants are required to fulfill the eligibility criteria established for the scholarship scheme.SC, ST, and OBC candidates applying for the National Overseas Scholarship must fulfill the requirements given below:Educational Qualification:For Masters:Minimum 55% or it's equivalent at the undergraduate level.For Doctorate:Minimum 55% or it's equivalent at the master’s levelApplicants must note that preference will be given to candidates with relevant experience.Maximum Age Limit:Applicants must be below 35 years as of April 1, 2025.Maximum Permissible Income Limit:Total family income should not exceed 6,00,000 INR.Children Criteria:Only two children from the same parent can get the benefit of the National Overseas Scholarship.Along fulfilling these requirements, it is significant that the applicants meet the minimum score requirements of tests likeIELTS,PTE,GMAT, GRE, etc. Every university has its own minimum ELP and other test score requirements.Note:Bachelor Degree courses are NOT ELIGIBLE for the scholarshipCandidates already settled or studying abroad will not be considered for the scholarshipA maximum of two candidates from a single-family are allowed to avail this scholarship | Application: The application form for the National Overseas Scholarship will be made available from the Secondweek of Febrauryon the official website of the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.Students are required to submit the completed application along with necessary documents within the established deadline. The Ministry requires all the students to submit self-attested copies of the following documents:Proof of Date of BirthAll Degree/ Diploma/ Certificate along with mark sheetsIncome Certificate of FamilyOffer Letter College/ UniversityNote:Offer of admission from the chosenuniversity in Germany, Canada, US is mandatory. | Selection: The Selection Committee for the National Overseas Scholarship considers all applications impartially. The selection for the scholarship award is given to the candidates who fulfill the eligibility criteria.Along with that, the National Overseas Scholarship panel focuses on the following factors:Only applications submitted before the deadline will be considered.Preference will be given to those who have already secured admission at auniversity in the UK, Germany, Canada, etc.Students yet to secure admission to an international university will also be considered on a conditional basis.In case of a tie, between two or more applicants, the following procedure will be followed:First of all, available unfilled slots earmarked for female applicants will be given to the most eligible candidates.Next, the remaining slots will be filled up by the eligible applicants exceeding the age limit, i.e., the first available slot will be given to the oldest candidate and subsequent slots in the descending order.Note:Applicants must remember thatNOadditional interviews or group discussions will take place.

Firstly, to be eligible for the scholarship, candidates need to be from the list of the countries mentioned eligible for the scholarship. The candidates are supposed to perform outstandingly well in their undergraduate degrees so that their application can be considered by the award distributing committee.Except for the above-mentioned points, candidates need to fulfill the eligibility criteria as defined by the university:Secureadmission at the University of Sussexin the MBA program.Previous senior management experienceEligible CountriesStudents from Bangladesh, India, Bhutan, China, Egypt, England, Japan, France, Germany, Malaysia, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, Panama, Sri Lanka, and a few more countries are eligible for the award. | Application: The application forms for the Sussex MBA scholarship are available on the official website of the University of Sussex for the September session. The scholarship forms will be accepted via online process. Candidates are supposed to first enroll themselves in theUniversity of Sussex’s MBA programusing the university’s PG online application system.Admission RequirementStudents are required to submit their updated CV and a personal statement.At the time of admissions, all the students must have a lower second-class undergraduate honors degree.Candidates are supposed to have their IELTS score with them for being eligible to take admissions for MBA at the University of Sussex.Note : Once a decision has been reached whether you will be awarded a scholarship you will be notified of the outcome. Should you be offered a scholarship you have to accept/decline the offer within one month. You will not be able to defer the scholarship offer to a different start date.DeadlinesFor starting in September 2026:30 September 2026For starting in January 2027:30 November 2026 | Selection: The major decisions related to the Sussex MBA scholarship are taken based on the criteria defined by the university:Relevant academic qualificationManagerial work experienceAdmission offer to MBA programThe university ensures that all eligible applicants receive benefits of the scholarship program.Note:Students who donot possessup to the mark results in the undergraduate degree and lack of relevant management experience can also apply for the scholarship by writing an informal application of interest atmba@sussex.ac.uk.For these students,an interview sessionwill be conducted.Once the decision has been made by the committee regarding the distribution of the scholarship, as per the defined parameters, the student is notified for the same and is supposed to accept or decline the offer, within one month. As soon as they accept the offer, students mustapply for Tier 4 UK Student visa.

To become eligible for BrokerFish International student scholarship, there are some general requirements that the students applying for both postgraduate and undergraduate courses need to fulfill:The applicant must be pursuing a degree, diploma, or equivalent program from an accredited university.It must be kept in mind, that employees of academic institutions and their respective family members are not eligible for this scholarship | Application: The interested applicants must apply for this scholarship program in December in order to be a part of this program. The applicants must note that they have to submit required documents on the basis of which they will be assessed. The required documents are as follows:The applicant must submit an essay of 500 – 1000 words on the topic entitled“The Importance of International Health Insurance for Expatriates”.The essay must focus on why international health insurance is important for anyone who is staying away from their home country for studying, working, or for any other purpose.A photograph is required that captures the thrust of the essay that the applicant has written.Proof of admissionmust be submitted along with the above documents in order to prove that you are a qualified student.All these documents must be sent via mail to the BrokerFish on the mail idscholarships@brokerfish.comwith the title as“BrokerFish(year) Scholarship. | Selection: After the applicant has successfully applied for the scholarship, next comes the selection process.The selection of the candidate is done onmerit-basis.Profile of the potential candidate is evaluated so that no deserving student is missed out and the most deserving one gets the reward.Selected students will be intimated through email and will be awarded the scholarship amount of1000 USD, which he/she can use only for covering health insurance expenses.

Giri Global Excellence Scholarship, applicants are expected to fulfill the eligibility requirements given below:Classified as an international student (non-EU) for tuition fee and application purposes.Should be a self-funding student.Must belong to eligible countryApplied for a full-timeUCD graduate-taught or undergraduate program.College of Arts and HumanitiesCollege of BusinessCollege of Engineering and ArchitectureCollege of Health and Agricultural SciencesCollege of Social Sciences and LawProof of English language proficiency throughIELTSorTOEFL.Obtained a conditional/ unconditional offer of admission to the university.V.V. Giri Global Excellence ScholarshipRegional EligibilityThe applicants from the following countries are eligible for the V.V. Giri Global Excellence Scholarship tab.Step IV:Answer the general questions and submit the form.Applications for the scholarship are to be made this way only. Giri Global Excellence Scholarship Application. Scholarship decision is based primarily on theacademic meritof the candidates.A holistic review of the scholarship application will be taken into consideration.Scores of theexams to study in IrelandCompletion of application on timeReference and statementsA face-to-face or virtual interview of the candidate with a Scholarship Committee member is conducted before the final decision is taken.Recipients of the V.V. Giri Global Excellence Scholarship will be notified within 2 to 4 weeks. Students must accept their scholarship offer within the timeframe indicated in the letter/email. The eligibility requirements for the Orange Tulip Scholarship are quite basic and straightforward. The most important part is the candidates must be Indian citizens not working in the Netherlands.Applicants who fulfil the requirements mentioned below can proceed to apply for the scholarship:Candidates need to be Indian citizens not currently employed orstudying in the Netherlands.Currently applying or already enrolled in one of the premier universities in the Netherlands Orange Tulip Scholarship participants.ShouldNotbe a part of any otherinternational scholarships in the Netherlands. | Application: Applicants must be accepted at any of the eligible institutes for a full-time program or should be in the process of applying foruniversities in the Netherlandsto avail Orange Tulip Scholarship.How to apply for the Orange Tulip Scholarships?The step-by-step process for the application is given below:Establish OTS eligibility and entry requirements.Choose an appropriate course as well as Netherlands college from the Nuffic NESOs Study Finder portal.Apply and get confirmation from the chosen Dutch UniversityComplete the Orange Tulip Scholarship application process by downloading the OTS form from Nesco.Submit the form in word or PDF format (file size should be below 20 MB)Attach copies of required documents.The candidates must email the duly filled application form toots@nesoindia.org.The subject of the email should be: OTS 2024-2025 Application [scheme] - [your name].Example:OTS 2024-2025 Application-Radboud University- Shreya Dey.The filename of each document should have a clear name in English: [document name] - [applicant’s name]. Example:IELTSScore Sheet- Shreya Dey. | Selection: The selection of the Orange Tulip Scholarship India is based on the academic performance and work experience of an applicant. The selection process for the OTS India program includes the following stages:Phase 1: Application Acceptance & Pre-SelectionNeso India will select the highly talented candidates on the basis of the general terms and conditions mentioned on the first page along with the specified requirements set by the Orange Tulip Scholarship benefactor. Only the files which are complete in all respects and attaining the minimum specifications will be sent to the Company or University for necessary formalities.Phase 2:File Review and Selection of RecipientsTo evaluate the overall process of file registration and selection by the Orange Tulip Scholarship benefactor. Will choose the registration file that qualifies the pre-selectionphase. This phase is basically done by the sponsor.Stage 3:Announcement of Scholarship RecipientsEach OrangeTulip Scholarship benefactor will declare the results about selection to Nesco India or directly inform the successful student. If the selected candidate receives the Scholarship Offer, the offer letter must be endorsed and returned to Neso India.The next phase regarding scholarship agreement, tuition fees will be conducted between the scholarship recipient and the sponsor.

To be eligible for the Deakin India 20% Undergraduate Scholarship, candidates must enroll in full-time bachelor-levelprograms at Deakin University. There are no age-based restrictions.In addition to the aforementioned criteria, an applicant has to fulfill the following conditions:Must be an Indian residentMust be living in India at the time of application.The application must be made through a Deakin University authorized agent officially licensed in India*.Must have an average score of 70-79.9% in XII, as listed by the National Office for Overseas Skills Recognition (NOOSR).If you have a score of 80% or above in XII, then you are eligible forDeakin India 25% merit scholarship.*There are more than 140 Deakin representatives in India located in Kerala, Ahmedabad, New Delhi, Ludhiana, Vijayawada, and many other states.Note:Non-Indian applicants must fulfill the following eligibility criteria:Must have above average marks of 60-64% in class XII.Must be living in the country of birth. | Application: The Deakin India 20% of Undergraduate Scholarship application should be made online with the help of Deakin's Overseas Representative. Along with the scholarship application, students are required to submit essential academic documents.Student DocumentationSome of the essential supporting documents are mentioned below:English Language proficiency proof (IELTS/TOEFL/CPE/CAE/PTE)Certified academic transcripts.There might be an interview.Copy of passport.Curriculum Vitae (Encouraged but not essential)Personal statement(might be needed)Scholarship applicant ID (if available)Note:Documents must reach the university before the closing date. Documents must be translated into English.

The Ritchie-Jennings Memorial Scholarship Program covers students who are actively seeking education and have a desire to pursue a career in fraud examinations. Many of the scholarship holders have dignified careers in the anti-fraud profession. Students who fulfill the requirements given below are eligible for the Ritchie-Jennings Memorial Scholarship Program:Enrolled in an accrediteduniversity/college in the U.S.Declared their major or minor in either business administration, accounting, finance or criminal justice.Demonstrated a substantial desire to pursue a career in a fraud examination or a likewise profession.CompletedRitchie-Jennings Memorial Scholarship application, supported by the official transcripts and other documentsUndergraduate Students:Must be enrolled for 9 semester hours per term.Graduate Students:Must be enrolled for 6 semester hours per term.Must have submitted two recommendation forms.Note:The applicants will provide information on two recommenders. These individuals will upload information forms on the applicant's behalf. At least one form is preferred to be from a Certified Fraud Examiner or a professor. During the evaluation process, admission/selection committee verify the following details:Completion of relevant UoLIC Pre-master’s programCumulative percentage obtained in the programChoice of UoL full-time masters degree programExams taken to study in the UKPrevious academic qualificationsThe universityguarantees scholarship awardto the applicants who meet the eligibility requirements and complete the application process on time.

scholarship_name: Accounting and Finance Division Top Ten Achievement Scholarship
detailed_eligibility: Accounting and Finance Division Top Ten Achievement Scholarship is designed for continued UG students. The eligibility criteria for this scholarship program are:Applicants should be pursuingBSc Accounting & Financeor BSc Banking & Finance at Leeds University Business School.Applicants should either be the first-year or second-year students.Applicants must demonstrate outstanding academic excellence during their studies.The scholarship program is available for both domestic and international students. However, applicants are advised to submit proof of residency for verification. | Application: The Accounting and Finance Division Top Ten Achievement Scholarship does not have any additional application process. Students are reviewed based on the applications submitted at the time ofadmissions at the University of Leeds Business School.Admission Requirements for International Students:Academic records/transcriptsProof of English proficiency for non-native English speakers.IELTS: 6.0TOEFL-iBT: 87One Letter of recommendationAn updated resumeLetter of Intent or Statement of purpose | Selection: The selection for Accounting and Finance Division Top Ten Achievement Scholarship is based on theacademic performanceof the students. The top 20 students who have scored the highest marks in the Accounting and Finance Division in the first and second years will be selected.The students achieving higher grades in the first year will be awarded in the second year and the students who are achieving higher grades in 2nd year will be awarded in the third year. How the scholarship will be awardedIt will be awarded in the next year, after the year of achievement.Students planning to spend their third year in an industry or study abroad will be receiving the award at the starting of the third year.Students who become temporary leavers for a valid reason, such as ill-health, will also have their award deferred until their return to study.

scholarship_name: Hira and Kamal Ahuja Award in Engineering
detailed_eligibility: Eligibility & selection criteria:Graduate students registered full-time in a Master's or Doctoral program in the Faculty of EngineeringApplicants with minimum 80% aggregate are eligibleInternational students who have graduated from any of the following IITs – Delhi, Mumbai, Ropar, Roorkee, Kanpur, and Kharagpur. If an eligible candidate from IIT is not available, students from all NIITs in India will be considered.Permanent resident of Canada who has graduated from IIT or NIITPreference will be given to candidates who identify as women,who fulfill the above criteria, and wherein women are underrepresentedShould an eligible candidate not be available in any one year, available funds will be held and multiple awards may be given out in a future year Engineering Graduate Office in consultation with Prof. Sanjeev Bedi will identify candidates and select recipients. | Application: Candidates are required to follow the steps to apply for Graduate Award Application are listed below:Complete theGraduate Student Award applicationAttach required documentation as mentioned in the application form.Submit your completed application package to Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs (GSPA) before the application deadline.Students who wish to avail of the benefits of scholarship should also know about theadmission process at the University of Waterloo.Application to Waterloo (by International Students) can be made through OUAC (Ontario Universities Application Centre). Before filling this form, candidates must check whether they meet the admission requirements or not.Within 2 weeks of receiving the application, the university will send instructions for further steps. These instructions will be sent through email, from the address myapplication@waterloo.ca.After this, candidates will need to create a Guest Account on the university servers. Candidates then need to upload the required documents and will help them check the application status.Candidates can then use the ‘Application Information Form’, to further strengthen the chances of their admission.International Candidates have to upload the official transcripts of their academic certificates, along with their English Proficiency Test Scores.Selected candidates will be sent the application offer.Candidates can log in to their OUAC account to check the offer. Candidates need to accept the offer within deadlines given, to be considered for further process.Selected (international) candidates then need to apply for Study Permit and submit it to the university after they get it. | Selection: The selection process takes place once the application is received by the university.The application is mandatory for the selection process to take place.Student applications with good academic results and demonstration of their cultural responsibility to the Indian community will be considered first and will also stand a chance to get selected into the university soon.The selection process will take place with the application review followed by the interview.Selected candidates will be intimated through email.

scholarship_name: Monash Business School Undergraduate Scholarship- India
detailed_eligibility: All international students are eligible to apply for Monash College India - Monash Business School Undergraduate Scholarship. In addition to this, Candidates have to meet the eligibility criteria as defined by the university:Must be commencing an undergraduate program in the Monash Business School, Australia campus.Must have completed the international pathway India program.Must have scored a minimum weighted average mark* of 75.*Weighted average mark means the average of marks earned in all completed units of a program. It is a popularly used method by the universities to judge the academic performance of a candidate. It may also include the marks achieved in extracurricular activities or project work. The weights are variable and assigned by the university itself. It is calculated by using the credit point for each unit and the year level. | Application: The Monash College India - Monash Business School Undergraduate Scholarship is awarded on the basis of merit so no application process is required. The scholarship is for the students who take admission in the undergraduate program at Monash University.The academic records and performance of a candidate are reviewed which was submitted at the time of application foradmission at Monash College. The Monash College is fully administered by theMonash University. | Selection: The selection of students for the scholarship is clearly dependent on the academic performance which they display in their application for admission. This scholarship is renewable for a period of 2 years but in order to retain it the students must meet the set criteria.The criteria for retaining the scholarship for another year is maintaining a minimum 65 weighted average mark while pursuing a bachelor’s degree in the college. The scholarship amount goes up to20,000 USDif the performance of the candidate is outstanding in the second year as the shortlisted candidates get the amount in installments of10,000 USDfor two years.

scholarship_name: Abdul Majid Bader Graduate Scholarship- Dalhousie University
detailed_eligibility: To get the Abdul Majid Bader Graduate Scholarship, students are first supposed to take admissions in Masters or Doctoralprograms at Dalhousie University. Apart from that, candidates are supposed to meet the eligibility criteria as defined by the university.Students from all over the world can apply for this Scholarship.Iranian students are given first preference.Students must have maintained at least a 3.7 GPA on a scale of 4.3 GPA in the last two years of their academic studies.Students are eligible for the complete duration of the programs upon renewal.Students from any discipline are eligible.The eligibility criteria stated by the university are purely based on merit which makes it easy for bright students to get an opportunity tostudy in Canada. | Application: If candidates meet the eligibility criteria, they are supposed to fill the application form for theAbdul Majid Bader Graduate Scholarship.Faculty of Graduate Studies at Dalhousie University followHarmonized Scholarship Processfor awarding the scholarship. This process uses a common application form for various FGS Scholarships.There are two ways to apply for this Scholarship:Current students or new students once they have applied for a program at the Dalhousie University, can apply directly before the Internal Deadline of their Department.Departments can nominate eligible students and submit the nominated students’ paperwork.Applicants need to provide the following documents:Completed application formMasters -Click hereDoctoral -Click hereTranscripts from previously attended universities. Final discretion will depend until the original transcripts are submitted.Two Reference lettersApplication forms should be sent by post. | Selection: Applications are evaluated on the basis of Tri-Council Guidelines for evaluation of academic excellence, research ability, communication, interpersonal and leadership abilities.Masters applications are evaluated through CGS-M criteriaIt considers applicants’ background in academic excellence - Scholarships and awards, duration of previous studies, type of program and courses pursued, etc.Research Potential-Quality and Originality of contribution to research and development, judgment and ability to think critically, ability to apply skills and knowledge, significance, the feasibility of proposed research, etc.Personal Characteristics and Interpersonal Skills-Work experience, leadership experience, volunteerism, ability to communicate theoretical, technical and Scientific concepts, etcDoctoral applications are evaluated through CGS Doctoral Criteria.Research Ability and Potential -Quality of Research Proposal, Relevant training, quality of contributions and extent of advancement in research, ability to communicate theoretical and scientific concepts, etcRelevant Experience and Achievements obtainedwithin and beyond academia- Scholarships and awards, academic record, professional and extracurricular activities, etc.Offers are communicated to the respective departments.Applicants will have 21 days to accept or reject the offer.Note:The tuition fee is not waived by the scholarship and the students are supposed to pay the same with the scholarship amount. Application for the scholarship should be submitted before the internal deadline of the particular department.

The Brilliant Minds MBA scholarship will be awarded to students who opt for MBAprograms at the Imperial College Business School. Apart from that, students need to meet the eligibility criteria set by the college:Applicant must have a strong academic background, high scores in GMAT and also possess excellent professional record before joining the college.Applicants will have to submit GMAT scores before the deadline. The college expects these scores to not only be high but also well balanced.Applicants will be assessed during the admission process to judge eligibility on academic and professional grounds.Applicants will have to submit professional references along with their applications. The college may contact them if they find it appropriate.It is preferable for the candidates to hold either a first-class honors degree or its international equivalent. | Application: The applications for full-time MBA which will be received by the college before the deadline with all the necessary requirements shall be automatically considered for the Brilliant Minds MBA Scholarship. The application requirements and process are described below:Complete an application form for admission to a full-timeMBA program at Imperial College Business School.SubmitGMATscores.Submit an essay as a part of the application. All candidates will have to write a 700-word essay on the topic: “Describe your greatest academic achievement, and why this is a particular source of pride for you.”Candidates are recommended to submit their application before the deadline. This helps in increasing the chance of getting the scholarship. The deadline for scholarship applications are as follows:The completed application along with the essay can be submitted either as a part of the application form or via email to mba@imperial.ac.uk within the deadlines mentioned above. Any emails regarding this scholarship must state theBrilliant Minds Scholarship applicationand the candidate’s CID number should be mentioned in the subject line. | Selection: The selection for awarding scholarships to candidates is made by two scholarship committees based upon the date of submission of application.The full-time MBA program commences in September. Candidates who receive an offer for admission become a part of the selection process for the scholarship. The selection is made on the basis of the academic standing that the candidate displays at the time of application, the balance of GMAT scores and the response in the form of an essay.Also, the professional experience of the candidate and how their professional references speak of them will also be considered for the selection process. Therefore, it is completely a merit-based scholarship.

London Business School India Scholarship program is specifically designed for the Indian students, who show their academic excellence and get admission to theMBA program at the London Business School.Only one student, out of the many who apply for this gets the scholarship award.Therefore, the students must fulfill the requirements given below to get eligible for the London Business School India Scholarship 2021:Nationality:Indian citizensWork Experience:A minimum of 2 years of experience in the related field.Along with this, the applicant has to meet academic requirements for the ScholarshipEnglish Proficiency scores:IELTSor PTEELP can be exempted, if the UG or PG degree of the applicant was in English or if he/she has worked or lived in an English speaking country (min 2 years)Exam Scores:GMAT/GREScoresBachelor degree or equivalent qualification | Application: Applicants do not need to fill any kind of application form for the scholarship, they will be automatically considered for this award if they satisfy the eligibility criteria. Therefore, all an applicant has to do is secureadmission at LSB.Some other documents required to submit to avail benefits of the London Business School India Scholarship are :Two Letters of Recommendation.An essay on the Post MBA goals of the student.An updated CV detailing work experience in a related field. | Selection: Candidates are shortlisted based on the eligibility criteria and submission of required documents. Shortlisted candidates are then invited for an interview session.The interviews are conducted by an alumnus of the school’s MBA program. Interviews are conducted in India. Candidates may have to travel for the interview.The interview session helps in evaluating the applicant’s abilities to work in multicultural teams and an international environment, along with the eligibility parameters. The result and recipient’s name is declared on the London School of Business’ official website.

As per the eligibility criteria for the Eiffel Excellence Scholarship, candidates are not supposed to fulfill any special requirement. However, candidates need to meet the basic eligibility criteria as defined by the scholarship awarding body:The candidate must be an international student and submit a copy of the passport for verification.Applications must be submitted via French education institutions. Any application submitted through other sources will not be entertained.Applicants must be opting for a Master's level or Ph.D. program.Programs eligible for the scholarship:Political ScienceLawEconomics and managementEngineering SciencesTerms and Conditions for Eiffel Excellence Scholarship ProgramApplicants must not have already applied for any scholarship provided by the French government.Students who are applying for the second time will not be entertained even if the institution and course are different.Students should also have a basic knowledge of the French language.Note:Students who got the scholarship at the Masters's level canre-apply for receiving the scholarshipat a Ph.D. level. | Application: The applications for the Eiffel Excellence Scholarship program are not accepted directly by the Students. All the applications from the students for the scholarship are to be submitted by the French Higher Education Institutions.Check more scholarshipsBefore applying for the Eiffel Excellence Scholarship, applicants are advised to go through the below-mentioned points:Students are supposed to contact the International relations department of the university via email, phone or website to know the deadlines.Students are supposed to contact the French Embassy office or the Campus France office in their home country.The respective French university supports the application form and helps the student in completing the form.The application if finally send by the university on behalf of the student. | Selection: The selection process for the Eiffel Excellence Scholarship is carried out by a committee consisting of four panels of experts from the department of political science, law, engineering sciences, and management.The members of the panels are not allowed to evaluate the applications form the institutions with which they are associated or affiliated.Candidates are judged based on their academic performance as mentioned in their application forms.The institution and the department of the candidates are also considered based on their contribution to promoting the Eiffel Excellence program.Around 70% of the scholarships are offered to the candidates with outstanding academic performance and the rest 30% of scholarships are awarded to the candidates who have not received any scholarship.

scholarship_name: Karen McKellin International Leader of Tomorrow Award
detailed_eligibility: In order to be considered for the Karen McKellin International Leader of Tomorrow Award, applicants must fulfill all the requirements listed below:Nationality: International student studying in Canada on a verifiedCanadian student visa.High School Completion:From a recognized secondary school/ college, or another university.Academic Qualification:Demonstrate exceptional and consistent academic performance.UG Condition:Securedadmission at the University of British Columbia.Students should be entering their first year of the UBC undergraduate degree.Demonstrate financial need that will otherwise prevent the applicant from studying in Canada.Applicants must note that if they are nominated for a Karen McKellin International Leader of Tomorrow Award, then they will not be considered for merit-based awards offered by the university, includingInternational Major Entrance Scholarshipsand any other awards including theOutstanding International Student Awards. | Application: The process for the Karen McKellin International Leader of Tomorrow Award is given below:Mid- September:Decide on your academic program/campus.Late September:Decide on a nominator/referee.Early October:Discuss your nomination and create an account:-The nominator and teacher recommendations need to be completed and submitted a few days beforeNovember 15.Early October: Start your UBC admissions application.Mid-October: Gather relevant information for application and complete your sections of the award application.By November 15: Submit your completed application.By December 1:Apply to UBC.By January 31:Submit required documents.Mid- April: Check your email to learn if you have received an award.ELP Test NameMinimum Score RequirementIELTS6.5 (no section below 6.0)PTE(Academic)Overall: 65; Reading: 60; Listening: 60; Writing: 60; Speaking: 60TOEFLOverall: 90; Reading: 22; Listening: 22; Writing: 21; Speaking: 21DuolingoOverall: 125Applications for 2026 open in mid-September. When the application opens, you can only apply to winter session (September 2026 – April 2027). | Selection: Students are awarded on the basis of their academic merit and financial need. In addition to that, the Karen McKellin International Leader of Tomorrow Award recipients are selected on the following factors:Involvement in the Student AffairsLeadership SkillsContribution to Community ServiceAchievement in athletics, performing art, creative writing or debating.Note:For scholarship, you must apply first for an eligibleprogram at the UBCand should fill the application form along with it, within the deadlines.

scholarship_name: University of Sussex Chancellor's International Scholarship
detailed_eligibility: This scholarship is available to all the students who plan to continue their higher education in the U.K. The students who have been grantedadmission to the University of Sussexcan avail this scholarship. To apply for the Chancellor’s International Scholarship, the students must fulfill the given criteria:The student must have accepted a place on a full-time eligible Masters, which will commence at the University of Sussex in September 2026.A student must have secured excellent grades (UK first class or equivalent)A student must have a clear and specific goal with defined links to the course. | Application: Applying for Chancellor’s International Scholarship is quite easy. To be considered for the Chancellors International Scholarship, the students have to received and accepted an offer of a place on an eligibleMasters at Sussex.To apply for the scholarship, the students need to submit a statement of academic interests and should provide clear information to the university about:Drive and ambitionsApplicant’s ambitious plan post completing his/her studies on returning to home countryRelevant work experience, skills or extra-curricular activities that relate to applicants’ ambitions and/or chosen course.In order to get considered for the scholarship, you need to showcase excellent grades in your most recent qualification.Also, the students have to submit the application online only asno offline forms are available.The applicant has to fill personal details like name, contact information, education qualification, background details.In case the students are applying for a master’s course which starts in September 2026, they mustsubmit the application by May 1, 2026. | Selection: The criterion of selection is that an applicant must meet the eligibility criteria set by the University.The student must have submitted the application form along with a statement by the mentioned deadline.The write up mentions the applicant’s ambition plans.An international student has to meet English language requirements or should have completed a pre-sessional English course.The criteria for getting selected for a scholarship also include performance in the interview session.Students will be evaluated based on the information provided in the application form. Information provided with the master’s application may be used as part of the assessment.The scholarship Office will contact the applicant after the final decision is taken. The application of non-eligible candidates will be withdrawn.

Candidates are required to meet the eligibility criteria given below to enjoy the benefits of the scholarship.Applicant must hold or be on track to obtain, at least a high 2:1 (65+%) in their undergraduate degree, including 60+% in modules relevant to the MSc program.Applicants must be from countries like Bangladesh, India, Nepal, Maldives, Pakistan and Sri Lanka only.Indian subcontinent applicants across all Liverpool on-campus MSc programs are eligible .Work experience relevant to the program would be advantageous, as would previously studying at a highly ranked university.Note 1:Work experience can include a paid internship, shadowing or voluntary positions.Note 2:All these criteria are for MSc taught program and not MSc research programs. | Application: Eligible program applicants will be considered. No separate scholarship application is needed. Documents required at the time of admission intoMSc programs at the University of Liverpoolare :School or College transcripts/certificatesUniversity transcripts and certified translation if applicableDegree certificatesEvidence of English Language proficiencyPersonal StatementTwo referencesThe application process might require an interview even if it is not otherwise mentioned to evaluate some candidates personally. A great positive attitude and good knowledge of the subject studied is generally advised to possess while giving the interview.Note:Scanned copies are allowed but students have to bring original documents at the time of admission. | Selection: Early program applications are advised as scholarships will be allocated on a first-come, first-served basis.The selection committee will review the program application along with documents required to submit for the scholarship. Selected candidates will be informed through email and scholarship funds will be allocated to them.

The University of Cambridge has established certain eligibility criteria that the prospective applicants need to fulfill. To be considered for the Dr. Manmohan Singh Scholarship program, an applicant must:Be an Indian national with a valid Indian Passport.Must be currently residing in IndiaHold a recognized masters of PG degree from an Indian university/institution.Below the age of 35 years as of January 31.Be intending to pursue a full-time Doctoral program at the University of Cambridge.For the 2021 scholarship, an applicant must be commencing the program in September-October 2021.Not be a recipient of otherscholarships to study in the U.K.Eligible CoursesHere is the list of the Doctoral courses that are included in the Dr. Manmohan Singh Scholarship.Science and TechnologyEconomicsSocial studiesAerospace EngineeringMarine EngineeringEnergy studies | Application: Applicants who fulfill the eligibility criteria can move ahead and apply for the scholarship. The application process of Dr. Manmohan Singh Scholarship is entirely online.Step-by-Step ProcessBefore applying for the scholarship, applicants must be complete applications to secureadmission at the University of Cambridge. The step-by-step process for Dr. Manmohan Singh Scholarship 2020 is given below:Step I:Choose a doctoral program and a supervisor at the University of Cambridge.Step II:Complete the admission requirements for theCambridge doctoral programs:UG and PG degrees and transcript2 academic/professional referenceResumeResearch proposalStatement of purpose/ personal essaySubmit the application forms before the admission application deadline.Step III:Choose St. John’s College as the first preference in the application form.Step IV:Upon confirmation of admission, complete the Dr. Manmohan Singh Scholarship application via the college website to notify the officials at St. John’s. The college will email the acknowledgment recipient to the applicants.Important DatesLast Date of application15thof January 2024Interview RoundBetween April 2024- June 2024Result DeclarationJune 2024Commencement of ClassesGenerally, in Sep/Oct every yearNote:Applicants must not contact St. John’s College at this stage. | Selection: Cambridge Universityfollows a strict selection process to select candidates for the Dr. Manmohan Singh Scholarship. The candidates are selected in a two-stage process:Stage I:Core selection committee and the Central Board of the St. Johns College and the Cambridge university go through the submitted applications and release the initial list of shortlisted candidates.Stage II:The PI round whereskype interviewsof candidates shortlisted from the first round are taken and then the final list of grantees is released.The interviews will be conducted between April and June 2024. Within a month of the skype interview session, the selection committee announces the name of the Dr. Manmohan Singh Scholarship recipient. The decision of the selection committee is final and cannot be challenged.Post Selection:After selection, St. John’s College will coordinate with the shortlisted students for pre-departure, travel arrangements, andU.K. student visas.

To get the scholarship, students are supposed to takeadmission at the University College Dublinin the MSc program. This scholarship is to provide financial aid to students with exceptional academic records.The shortlisted students get a 50% tuition fee waiver and are also trained to represent the course and the institution at a global level when they enter the job industry. Students are required to meet the eligibility criteria as stated by the university.2:1 or 65% in bachelor’s degreeA minimum score of 630 points in the GMAT test, if you haven’t attempted the GRE test (GMAT score is a primary factor kept in mind by the bench of admissions):Verbal component score must at least be 30/51,The quantitative score no lower than 40/60;A score lying in 130-170 scoring scale for GRE:Minimum 158 out of 170GREVerbal ReasoningMinimum 160 out of 170 GRE Quantitative ReasoningMust have obtained a first-class honors degree (or equivalent) from a recognized institution.Secured a minimum overall score of 7.0 in the IELTS test, a score of 100/120 in the TOEFL test (or equivalent): only if applicable.Who isn’t eligible:Students opting for the below-mentioned courses will be considered ineligible for theMSc Merit-Based Scholarship for International Students:MSc in Biotechnology and BusinessMSc in Biotherapeutics and BusinessMSc in International Law and Business | Application: Candidates have to first apply for admission in any of the MSc programs at the University College Dublin.All the students are supposed to fill the application form for the scholarship available at the official website of University College Dublin.After filling all the necessary details in the application form, candidates are required to attach their GRE orGMATtest scores with the application form.Candidates will be supposed to fill the application form before the last date of application which is August 01, 2025. | Selection: The selection of the candidates for the scholarship is completely based on merit, academic performance, and eligibility criteria and resume with all the details about the students.

To get the B.J. Seaman Scholarship, all the international students are required to meet the eligibility criteria as defined by the University of Calgary. The applicant:Must have takenadmissions at the University of Calgaryin a full-time undergraduate program.Should be continuing their studies in the 2nd, 3rd or 4th year in any faculty.Should have completed the most recent fall and winter terms with at least 24 units.Disabled students will be accepted with 18 units.Should have aminimum GPA of 3.20in the most recent fall and winter terms.Terms and Conditions:The students entering an after-degree program in the fall term are not eligible for this scholarship. However, they will be eligible after completing the first year of their after-degree program.The supplemental exams, deferred exams, and special deferred exams or term work can affect the eligibility of the candidate for the B.J. Seaman scholarship. | Application: Students who have enrolled themselves in any of theundergraduate programs at the University of Calgarycan fill the application form for the scholarship. The students are supposed to apply for the B.J. Seaman Scholarship by visiting the official website of the University of Calgary.The applicant will have to sign in to their Study Center from the myUofC portal. Here is the direct link for theportal.On the home page, click on ‘My Financials’.Then scroll down to the Awards & Scholarship section.Click on ‘Apply for Undergraduate Award’.Ensure that the popups are enabled in the windows.The pop-up window will open on the screen. If it does not open then maybe the pop-ups are blocked in the windows.The new page will show all the scholarships or awards that are available and their application deadline dates.Apply for the B.J. Seaman Scholarship. | Selection: The selection of the candidate is purely based on academic merit. The scholarship is only for the students enrolled in the undergraduateprograms at University of Calgary. The performances of the applicants in their most recent fall and winter terms will decide whether they will be selected for the scholarship or not.Few other things are considered such as the financial need, extracurricular activities, leadership, volunteerism, etc may be considered, depending on the terms of the scholarship.

scholarship_name: Young Women in Public Affairs Award
detailed_eligibility: Eligibility criteria to be selected for Young Women in Public Affairs Award is given below :Only female candidates are eligible for this scholarshipYoung women in theage group of 16 – 19 yearsas on April 01 every year will be eligibleApplicants must be students at a school, college, or university in a Zonta district/region, or studying elsewhere butare citizens of a Zonta CountryApplicants have to show proof ofActive commitment to volunteerism.Paid or unpaid leadership experience in local government or workplace.Volunteer leadership achievements.Knowledge in Zonta International and its programs.Support in Zonta International's mission of empowering women worldwide through service and advocacy.The applicant must not be a previous recipient of this award.Classified members, employees of Zonta International and Zonta International Foundation are not eligible to apply.** Classified members: Club members and individuals with direct membership with Zonta InternationalThe term ‘family members’ refer to - ancestors, descendants, adoptees, siblings, nieces or cousins, and spouse or cohabiting partner.Note: Z and Golden Z club members are also eligible to apply. | Application: Applicant must follow the steps given below to apply for Young Women in Public Affairs Awards:Step 1:Locate the nearest Zonta Club. *Step 2:Contact the Zonta Club for application deadline.Step 3:Download the application form from the website.Step 4:Fill the downloaded application form.Step 5:Submit required documents for verification of current enrolment from the school, college or university officials .Two confidential recommendationsfrom school, college or university are also required.Step 6:Submit all documents in English and the completed application form to your nearest Zonta Club.Note:Applicants from geographic areas within a Zonta district/ region where no clubs are located may apply through a Zonta e-club or directly to the district to be eligible for the district/ region and international awards.Application TimelineThe schedule of application and selection procedure is as follows:Date by which Zonta International posts the application on the official websiteAugust 01Last date by which Zonta clubs receive the applications.Varied (Contact club for deadline)Date by which club recipients are selected and applications are received by governor/ region representative.April 01Date by which district/ region recipients selected and applications are received by Zonta International Headquarters.May 01Announcement of district/ region and international winners.July 01 | Selection: All the applicants are shortlisted on the basis of their merit, concerning their commitment, volunteerism to public service and leadership skills.Each Zonta club reviews the submitted applications and the supporting documents and selects one applicant as the club level winner. The winning entry from each club is sent to the Zonta governor/ region representative.The district/ region evaluating committee reviews the applications received from various clubs and selects one applicant from each district/ region.The Zonta International Award Committee chooses 10 international recipients from district/region applications to Zonta International Board. After the consent of the Zonta International Board, Zonda Foundations providesYoung Women in Public Affairs Awardsand certificates to all the recipients.

To apply for VueVille Future Technology scholarship, students must fulfill the eligibility criteria mentioned below:Write a 750- 1000 words essay on “How Smart Home technology can help us adapt to the new normal of the COVID Era?”Enrolled as a full-time UG/PG student in anyuniversity abroadUniversities of the following countries will be considered:USACanadaAustraliaNew ZealandUKGermanySingaporeIndiaCompleting the chosen undergraduate or postgraduate degree. | Application: As mentioned, the application process for the VueVille Future Technology Scholarship comprises ofsubmission of the essay.For that, the applicants have to follow the guidelines given below:Write/copy your essay in Microsoft Word format.Mail it toscholarship[@]vueville.comalong with the necessary details.Provide personal details such asFirst & Last name, Age, Contact number, Mailing Address.Attach acertified letter from the chosen educationalincluding details like course name and student ID. | Selection: Students who want to avail of the benefit of the scholarship will submit an essay on the given topic: “?How Smart Home technology can help us adapt to the new normal of the COVID Era?”Applicants will be evaluated on the basis of the submitted essay only:Word count should be between 750- 1000 words.Essay must be original and creative; written after thorough research.The selection process for the VueVille Future Technology Scholarshipmay include aninterview session.The shortlisting process will begin in the month of October and the decision will be announced byNovember 30, 2020.The decision notification will be sent to the recipient’s educational institution.

scholarship_name: Global Academic Excellence Scholarship- University of Adelaide
detailed_eligibility: The student is considered for the scholarship upon application or enrollment in their desired field of study at UG and PG level. The applicant must meet all theadmission requirements at the University of Adelaidefor the course work. The application must contain all the required documents and must be error-free.The academic eligibility for the scholarship is:Raw ATAR of 98 OR 95% (for UG level)GPA 6.8on 7.0 OR equivalent (for PG level)Applicants who arenot eligibleto receive Global Academic Excellence Scholarship scholarships are:Students who are transferring from one program to another prior to the successful completion of their program of study (internal transfers);Students who are recipients of a scholarship awarded by recognized bodies such as a government ministry or department;Applicants for Masters by Research or Ph.D. programs. | Application: It is not necessary to apply for the University of Adelaide Global Academic Excellence Scholarship because this scholarship will be automatically awarded to students who meet the eligibility requirements. No separate Scholarship application form is required. Scholarship recipients will be confirmed at the point of acceptance.A list of eligible students will be provide to faculties for selection of scholarships. Successful applicants will be advised in writing within fourteen days of faculty approval.Satisfactory completion of Year 12 or AISSC/ ISC or GCE Advanced Level Examination for UG admissionsSatisfactory completion of Bachelors degree or its equivalent for PG admissionsStudents must provide test scores of ELP test likeIELTS,TOEFL, PTE or C1 Advanced | Selection: The selection process for the Global Academic Excellence Scholarship scholarship is competitive. The main criteria for scholarship selection are academic achievement.Students are expected to haveexcellent academic performancein their previous course work. Students are selected based on their scores at high school or undergraduate level.A student must have a score equivalent to that ofATAR 98 and GPA 6.8for UG and PG levels, respectively, in order to increase their chances of getting selected for the scholarship.Applicants who are eligible for more than one scholarship offered by the university will receive only one scholarship.

Students have to qualify for the respectiveexams to study abroadas it comes under the eligibility criteria for admissions in various institutes.
