Candidates are also required to meet the eligibility criteria as defined by the Hinrich Foundation to avail the scholarship:Should be either a citizen of Vietnam, Philippines, Indonesia or India.Should have 5+ years of working experience.Must have acquired at least an undergraduate degree in either marketing, business, journalism, social sciences, or any other related field.Should be proficient in English with a valid test score in support of such proficiency. Acceptable English proficiency test scores are:TOEFL (iBT):79IELTS6.5Must showcase strong communication & teamwork skills as well as leadership ability. | Application: Applications for the Hinrich Full Scholarship forMasters of International Journalismprograms at Hong Kong Baptist Universityhas to be submitted through the foundation’s online portal. The application should be supported by the following documentation:Academic transcriptsValid ELP ScoresUpdated Curriculum vitaeCover letterProfessional LORA copy of the PassportA 500-word essay on the topic “How will the Work Integrated Learning approach help you to become an outstanding Global Trade Leader?”Note:You can drop in your queries at careerdevelopment@hinrichfoundation.com. | Selection: Applications should be complete in all respect and should be submitted before the scheduled deadline to be considered for the scholarship program. Candidates who can demonstrate a clear understanding of study objectives and can show a connection between their academic rigor and the intended career through the essay and a compelling cover letter stands better scope of winning the award.Please notethat applicants who,demonstrate an interest in working in Asia after degree completion;exhibit a strong interest in working in trade-related sectors; and,agree to engage with the Hinrich Foundation post completion of the degree for mentorship as well as membership in the foundation’s Alumni Association;are given preference during the selection process of the Hinrich Full Scholarship for MAIJS in HKBU.

scholarship_name: Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program
detailed_eligibility: In order to avail the benefits of the Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program, applicants must be admitted to a master’s degree program.Applicants from developing countries must fulfill the eligibility criteria listed below:A national of a World Bank member of developing countryNot hold dual citizenship of any developed countryMust hold a Bachelor’s (or equivalent) degree.Must be in good healthMust be a full time paid employee in anydevelopment-related workwhile submitting the scholarship application.Three years of relevant experience in related areas is mandatory.Must have an unconditional admission letter to any one of the partner or preferred master’s programs offered by JJ/WBGSP.It can on or before the application deadline.An admission letter must be uploaded before submitting the scholarship application.Must have takenexams to study abroad.Executive Director, any staff member or any close relative is ineligible.Note:Information is subject to change between the close of one application process and the opening of the next. | Application: The Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program applications must be submitted online. JJ/WBGSP 2020 applications should includeTwo professional referencesAn unconditional offer letter from the Preferred/ Partner master’s degree program.While the application requirements are the same for both Preferred and Partner Programs, the application process varies.Preferred Master’s ProgramsAll an applicant has to do is complete the program admission form. After securing admission in any one of the preferred programs, an applicant can apply for the JJ/WBGSP scholarship application.Partner Master’s ProgramsAn applicant must apply for admission to one or more of the Partner Master's programs. After reviewing submitted applications, each Partner Master's Degree Program will shortlist eligible candidates.JJ/WBGSP Secretariat will then invite the eligible candidates to apply for the scholarship.Exact dates will be communicated in the fall 2025.The applicant will be notified three times via email during a successful application process.Applicants are advised to not fill the application form using mobile phones. | Selection: The selection of The Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program recipients is a rigorous process. The panel aims to identify candidates with the highest potential. It is significant to evaluate the recipients who will contribute to the development of their country after completing the graduate study.TheJoint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship ProgramSelection Step IEvaluation of application will be done by two qualified assessors. Rating will be given on a scale of 1-10 taking into accountfour main factors:Quality of Professional Experience (30% weight)Quality of Professional Recommendations (30% weight)Quality of Commitment to return to Home Country (30% weight)Quality of Education Background (10% weight)TheJoint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship ProgramSelection Step IIThe JJ/WBGSP Secretariat will take into consideration the average score given by two assessors and the following criteria to select the finalists:Maintaining a wide geographical distribution of awardsMaintaining distribution of awards across genderOffering scholarships to applicants who are in need of financial aid tostudy abroad.Unusual conditions, when judging employment experience and other aspects of an application.Recipients of the scholarship will be informed only after the approval of theJJWBG Steering Committee.This will take place aroundmid-July 2023.Successful applicants will be asked to submit a copy of passport for identification and health certificate (21 days after the scholarship has been granted)

scholarship_name: Innovation in Education Scholarship - La Tutors 123
detailed_eligibility: Eligible candidates should be outstanding students who have made a difference in the lives of others in some innovative or technological fashion.The students can apply even if they do not take services from the LA Tutors 123. Other Eligibility criteria that student must fulfill to gain benefits ofInnovation in Education Scholarshipare:Applicant must be currently enrolled as high school or college or a university as a student situated in the U.S. or Canada.The applicant must have a cumulative GPA of at least 3.0 or its equivalent.The innovative creation of the Applicant must be involved in any kind of innovation creation whether it is a website, blog posts, an app, or anything similarThe essay provided by the applicant must accurately describe the goal of the project.The applicant’s essay, idea or creation must be original.Immediate family members of La Tutors staff are not eligible for the scholarship.The applicant must apply before 11:59 pm PST on the 20th of every month. | Application: The applicant must meet the eligibility criteria for the scholarship. He/she can then proceed to fill the application form forInnovation in Education Scholarshipprovided on the official website of LA TUTORS 123.The applicant is required to provide his/her personal information like name, email address, phone number, and high school/college name.An applicant is required to submit the following documents :Essay: Theessay should bepersonalized, indicate the goal of the project and its future prospective. Also, applicants must provide the link of the content if it is a blog or an app.Letter Of Recommendation: It must evaluate the quality of the project.Photograph of applicant: It should be taken during project execution and should be in JPG, PNG, GIF, TIFF format only.One must upload all the files at once by clicking on the ‘Upload Scholarship Documents’ button. The completed application must be submitted on the20th of the particular monthin which the student wishes to participate. | Selection: The selection processfor Innovation in Education Scholarshipinvolves evaluation on the grounds of creativity, innovation and user experience of the project. As the project is supposed to be a product of the imagination and technical skills of the applicant.All the applications will be checked, assessed and reviewed by apanel of three judges, after the 20th of each month.The judgment will be based on certain aspects like how well the project solves the problem, how the applicant presents his/her point of view, how the project strives to cultivate outcomes that are original and future-oriented.All submissions must be uploaded no later than 11:59 pm PST on the20th of each month.The submission window will remain closed after the 20th of every month and reopen on the 1st of next month at 8:00 a.m.Scholarship winners will be contacted via email by LA TUTORS 123 and must respond within five days of the date of the email to accept the award. If no response is received within a given time, another recipient will be selected for the scholarship.

To enjoy the benefits QS Connect Master’s Scholarship, candidates have to fulfill the eligibility criteria given below :Accepting admission offer from a school by the time QS selects the winner in May.The scholarship reward amount must be used for tuition fees only.Must attend a QS Connect Masters event.Study at a master’s (non MBA) levelNote:QS reserves the full right to exclude applicants from the selection process if they don’t meet the eligibility criteria. | Application: The application process for QS Connect Master’s Scholarship involves the submission of an essay.Applicants have to write an essay in about250 wordson the topic mentioned on the official website of the QS program and upload it on the portal of the QS Scholarship.The Topic for the year 2020 as given on the website is :The topic is “Considering your long-term objectives and ambitions, how will your master’s degree prepares you for your future career?”The submission of the essay plays a crucial role in the selection process for the scholarship. Here are some key points which should be kept in mind while writing an essay:Candidates mustread the essay statementcarefully and identify the key terms.Candidates must understand themeaning of the key themesand what these ideas mean and explore the ideas well with their experiences in the essay.Candidates should try to fill their scholarship essays with keywords used in the scholarship statement. This willdemonstrate their commitment to addressing the questionbeing asked.Candidates should try to makean engaging start to the essay.They can, for instance,add a quote that relates to their intended PG courseand later elaborate that quote further in the main body.One should try to understand the criteria used by the scholarship committee to evaluate the application essays. This further helps one toframe the article in technical aspects.The criteria are usually based on the topic given and the keywords. The committee will evaluate the essay onhow well the skills are presented combined with the themes.How to Submit QS Connect Master’s Scholarship EssayThe candidate should indicate theirFull name,Scholarship title, andthe QS fair which he/she has attended(example: QS Connect Masters-London-March 2019) at the beginning of the essay.These words are not included in the total word limit.The latest deadline is yet to be announced. | Selection: The selection process for QS Connect Master’s Scholarship involves the evaluation of an applicant’s profile along with an essay submitted by them.Applicants may be further evaluated on the basis of other documents submitted by them. This includes :TranscriptCV or ResumeLetters of RecommendationThe submission of the essay is a crucial part of the selection process. Applicants should be more personal while writing an essay. They can include relevant details of past and present experiences, future goals and interests of an applicant in a particular area. The use of effective language will enhance the quality of the content.Academic excellence, overall presentation, the format of the essay and quality of content will decide thewinner of the QS Connect Master’s Scholarship.

To be considered for Dr. Phillip Donaldson Memorial Scholarship, applicants must be commencing aUG-level program at CQUniversity. To secure the AAUW International Fellowships,applicants have to fulfill the criteria established by the foundation. While selecting the recipients of AAUW International Fellowships, the selection committee evaluates the following detailsApplicants who are residing in their home country will receive special consideration.Intent to return to their home country after the degree awarded.Academic and/or professional qualifications.Test scores ofexam to study in the USA.Commitment to the advancement of women and girls in her home country is taken into evaluation.Quality and feasibility of the proposed plan of study or researchProvide proof of prior community and/or civic service in the home country.Financial need is considered.Demonstration of motivation for graduate study or research.Applicants should be from an underrepresented area of the country and/or type of university other than a top-level research institution.Note:Applications, supporting documents, recommendations, etc. become the sole property of AAUW and will neither be returned nor held for another year.

The scholarship invites applications from all students, irrespective of their nationality, who wish tostudy abroadat Imperial College London. scholarship_name: La Trobe College Excellence Scholarship- South Asia
detailed_eligibility: To apply for theLa Trobe College Excellence Scholarships, the applicant should fulfill the following eligibility criteria:Be a citizen and resident of India, Sri Lanka, Pakistan, Kenya, Nepal, Bangladesh or MauritiusBe applying for one of theLa Trobe International Scholarship (LTIS) list of eligible courses.Be 18 years old or older at the commencement of the course; or must live with a student guardian approved by the Department of Home Affairs until they turn 18 years of age.Have an overall WAM* achievement of 80% or above.Commence their studies at any of the La Trobe University Victorian Campuses in 2024.Meet the University's English language requirements and all other conditions stated on the Offer Letter.They should meet the English Language requirement.English language ExamsMinimum scores (UG)Minimum Scores (PG)IELTS(Academic)6.0 (6.0 in each component)6.5 (6.0 in each component)TOEFL (PBT)550, with 5 or above in TWE (Test of Written English)550, with 5 or above in TWE (Test of Written English)TOEFL (iBT)64 (13 in reading, 12 in listening, 18 in speaking and 21 in writing)79 (13 in reading, 12 in listening, 18 in speaking and 21 in writing)Pearson Test of English(PTE)5058 | Application: Applicants must submit a complete application for the course commencing in Semester 2 (July) or Summer 1 / Term 6 (November) intake of 2024 at La Trobe University - Victorian campuses.Applicants must also provide the following documents before the application closing date:A detailed resumeA personal statement of no more than 300 words or a 3-4 minute video to detail "Why you should be awarded with the La Trobe University Excellence Scholarship".Applicants who have already applied for Semester 2 or Summer 1/Term 6 of 2024 will be considered for the scholarship if they meet the eligibility criteria. scholarship_name: Susan R. Meisinger Fellowship For Graduate Study in HR
detailed_eligibility: Susan R. Meisinger Fellowship is designed foran academically outstanding student, who is enrolled in a Master’s Degree program related to HR like organizational development, MBA, psychology, etc and should have a 50% degree remaining at least. Also, candidates must be SHRM certified or SHRM members.Academic Eligibility for Susan R. Meisinger FellowshipProspective UG and PG applicants for Susan R. Meisinger Fellowship are expected to fulfill the following academic-based requirements:Academics:An excellent academic record with a GPA of more than 3.0 on a scale of 4.0 in the UG program and 3.5 on a scale of 4.0 in the PG program.Degree Requirement:The applicant should not have received any other Master’s degree in an HR-related field.Along with these academic requirements, applicants must fulfill the following requirements:Work Experience:A person should haveat least five yearsof professional experience andthree years of HR experience.University Selection:An applicant may choose anyuniversity to study abroad. However, there are some regulations for the selection of the university.If the university is in the USA, then it needs to be regionally accredited.If it is a university outside of the USA, then it must be accredited by the government’s higher education or any equivalent body.Note:There are no eligibility criteria for this fellowship based on race, sexual orientation, gender, color, religion, ancestry, citizenship or national origin, marital status because of the non-discrimination policy of the SHRM Foundation. | Application: Susan R. Meisinger Fellowship 2024, for Graduate Study in HR, invites application each year. As a prospective candidate, you have to complete the online Susan R. Meisinger Fellowship Application and submit it, along with all the documents.Student Supporting DocumentationApplicants must provide the following student supporting documents at the time of Susan R. Meisinger Fellowship application submission:Contact information and details of past work experiences and projected career path.Transcripts of Undergraduate college and Graduate college(if enrolled).The transcripts must be written in English.For transcript evaluation, the SHRM Foundation can request a person from any reputable service like World Education Services.If a student is not currently enrolled in any master’s program then, a copy of the acceptance letter needs to be submitted.Two reference letters highlighting the person’s values, work ethics, characters, and career accomplishments.Must be addressed to the “SHRM Foundation Scholarship Review Committee”.Should be written by professors, colleagues, or coaches.A copy of an updated CV or resumeOne letter of referenceOpensJan 9 2026 12:00 AM (EST)DeadlineMar 31 2026 08:00 PM (EST) | Selection: The selection depends on various factors, which determine a student's capabilities and achievements. The recipient of the Susan R. Meisinger Fellowship is selected based on factors like,Academic Excellence during an undergraduate degreeLeadership QualityStudents AchievementsCommunity ServiceWork experiencesFinancial requirementpotential to become a successful HR leaderCareer ObjectiveAs a result of the selection process, two students or professionals are awarded annually,one person is an award recipient of the previous year whose fellowship gets renewed for a year andThe other is the recipient of that year.T&C for Renewing Susan R. Meisinger FellowshipThe student must fulfill the given requirements to continue the receipt of the award for the next year, after achieving the year in his first year of Masters study:During the fellowship period, a recipient needs to work in the HR field and continue SHRM membership or HR certification.A candidate is expected to give back to the community by serving as a judge of SHRM scholarships, reviewer of SHRM publication, participation in any research studies or item-writing panel of SHRM Certification.If selected to receive the Susan R. Meisinger Fellowship, recipients will be required to provide proof of enrollment and college/university payment instructions. The SHRM Foundation will issue a check not to exceed $10,000 directly to the institution for payment of tuition and fees required for enrollment or attendance at the educational institution, or for fees, books, supplies, and equipment required for courses at the educational institution.Special ConsiderationsEach scholarship is a one-time award, renewable for a second year; up to $10,000 total. Previous applicants may reapply, but past scholarship recipients are ineligible.Full-time, part-time, online, and distance learning programs are acceptable.Scholarships are for college-degree programs only and may not be used to attend professional development seminars.Recipients should intend on submitting proof of enrollment no later than July for the institution to receive timely payment.

Eigibility Criteria for ApplicantsThe German Chancellor Fellowship programme finances people from any industry, as long as they have a desire towork on a self-sustaining projecttogether with a host they want in Germany. However, there are certain eligibility criteria to get selected for the fellowship. We’ve made a tally for your help here:Eligible Nationalities:India, China, Brazil, Russia, South Africa or the USA.Academic Interest:Work in a field such as politics, media, business, society, culture or administration.Academic Qualification:Undergraduate degree in a relevant academic field.Experience & Skills:Demonstration of leadership in some capacity.Language Requirements:English or/ and German language proficiency.Check:Language scores to study in Germany.Note:For the last few years, 25-30% of the applicants have received the fellowships.Eligibility for German Chancellor Fellowship HostSomeone who works in a private or public sector in Germany can help as a German chancellor fellowship host. Some other requirements for the Host are:Host institution or the advisor need to accept the fellows withtravel and accommodation facilities in Germany.A host needs to confirm that the applicant receives a recommendation letter for their application.Host needs to provide the applicant with a well-deserved position.In return, the host will become a part of the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation and will be able to avail the advantages of the sponsorship even after the end of that particular project. | Application: The application timeline for the German Chancellor Fellowship isMarch 15 to October 15of every year. Candidates who establish their eligibility must apply for the fellowship within this period only. The candidate must submit the necessary documents for the application online to the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation only.Documents RequiredLetter of Motivation: In this letter, the applicant needs to describe what kind of leadership experience they have, what motivates them and what are their future plans and goals.Plan of Project: The applicant has to make a structure of the project that they have discussed with their host before applying. They will have to define the pros of their project. They will also have to explain what are their plans to make a connection between their home country and Germany in the future.Extensive Statement:A statement needs to be provided where the host from Germany has agreed to mentor the candidate.Letter of Recommendation:At least 2letters of recommendationare required from individuals that give clear information about their academic, personal and professional background.Wondering where to submit documents?The online application for German Chancellor Fellowship will include where the applicants can upload the statement and recommendation letters. While applicants are required to submit the statement themselves, they must forward the link to their respective referees for recommendations. A confirmation mail is sent once all the asked documents are received by the authorities. | Selection: Since the deadline for the German Chancellor Fellowship is October 15, the applications are evaluated post that. The selection process is coducted inApril(of the following year) and take place intwo rounds: Shortlisting of Candidates (based on documents) and Interview of shortlisted candidates. The details of both the rounds are mentioned below:Round I:Shortlisting takes place primarily on the basis of theiracademic merit. Other factors taken into account while selecting recipients are:Knowledge and potential to successfully complete the ongoing research project.Leadership skills and prior professional/field experiencePotential to promote connections between Germany and (applicant’s) origin country.Round II:Shortlisted candidates are invited for an interview session in their respective countries. The interview comprises a group discussion and a personal interview. While theGD roundfocuses on the general topics, thePI roundfocuses on the applicant’s personality, knowledge and approach towards their respective research projects.

Ott Scholarship for Applied Statistics and Quality Management must fulfill eligibility requirements given below:Either enrolled or are planning to take admission in masters or doctorate program in any Canadian orUS Universities.Must study in the field of statistics and quality managementMust have GPA scores of 3.25 or higher on a 4.0 point scale or an equivalent score in any field of UG level.Students are considered ineligible for the scholarship if :Enrolled in online programs.Are relatives of the Governing Board or ASQ Statistics Division Council members.Previous award winners | Application: Students who want to secure the Ellis R. Ott Scholarship for Applied Statistics and Quality Management have to complete the application form.Along with application form, students must submit documents listed below:CVLOR from college professors.Academic TranscriptsEssay describing the career goals, educational qualifications, and reasons for seeking the scholarship. It should not be longer than one page, minimum size 10 point type.The complete application form must be sent to the address mentioned below no later than May 01:AddressDr. Lynne B. Hare Kraft Foods,Research -East 200 DeForest Avenue East Hanover, NJ 07936-1944.Strong LOR increases the chances of selection for the scholarship. The governing Board considers it seriously while reviewing the application of students.Note:LOR should also include comments on all-round abilities of a student with special emphasis on leadership quality in the field of applied statistics and quality management and comments on the education qualification of the students. | Selection: Applications of students are reviewed by the Scholarship Governing Board for the selection of students. The following factors are considered while selecting the candidates:Academic achievement, including honors courses.Career objectives of the student.Faculty recommendations.Credit is given to Industrial exposure including part-time work and internships undertaken by the student.

TheUniversity of Oxforddoes have any specific eligibility requirements for the Clarendon Scholarship. All students who apply for Oxford’s Master’s and DPhil degree in any study field are eligible to receive the scholarship.Candidate must be applying for a full-time or part-time degree program.Admission applications must be submitted before the relevant January deadline.Minimum GPA score of 3.7 out of 4.0.Oxford students who are currently studying a master’s degree at the university and plan to re-apply for DPhil are also eligible for the scholarship program. | Application: Apply to your course by theDecember or January deadline, whichever is relevant to yourcourse, to be considered for a Clarendon scholarship. There is no scholarship application - all applicants to degree-bearing courses are considered.Students from all academic fields are considered for Clarendon Scholarships. | Selection: While selections are mainly based on the academic merit and potential of the applicants, the criteria slightly vary depending on the subject area. Applications are assessed on the following factors:Academic Score:It is essential to have a first-class honors degree or its equivalent. A GPA of 3.7 or higher on the scale of 4.0 is recommended.Future Potential and Aptitude:Review of references and research proposal helps in determining an applicant’s contribution to the field of study.Student Motivation:Applicant’s commitment towards the desired course as stated in the personal statement and evaluation of the referee reports.

Chevening Scholarships are provided on the conditions that you meet the following eligibility requirements:Be a citizen of a Chevening-eligible country or territory:Only applicants from countries or territories approved by Chevening such as India, Bangladesh,Canada are eligible to apply.Commit to returning to your home country for at least 2 years after your scholarship ends:This ensures that scholars use their UK-acquired knowledge and skills to contribute to their home country.Have at least 2,800 hours of work experience:Equivalent to around 2 years of full-time work, this can include full-time or part-time jobs, internships (paid or unpaid), and voluntary work, completed after undergraduate studies.Hold an undergraduate degree that qualifies you for a UK master’s programme:You must have completed your degree at least 2 years before the Chevening application deadline.Apply to 3 different eligible UK university courses:You must apply to 3 eligible master’s programs and receive an unconditional offer from at least one before the Chevening document deadline.You are not eligible to apply if:you have received any UK Government-funded scholarship, including Chevening, in the past 5 years. Also,You must not be currently or recently (within the last 2 years) employed by His Majesty’s Government, British embassies or high commissions, the British Council, UK universities, or affiliated organizations.Also checkTop Universities in UK | Application: The most important part while apply for Chevening Scholarships is the key dates. Filling out the application before the deadline helps in reviewing you applications thoroughly.DateEvent5 August 2025Applications open at 12:00 UTC7 October 2025Applications close at 12:00 UTC / GMTFrom October 2025Applications are sifted against eligibility criteriaMid-Oct 2025 – Jan 2026Reading committees assess eligible applicationsMid-February 2026Shortlisted candidates invited for interviewsMarch – April 2026Interviews held at British embassies and high commissions worldwideBy end of interview periodReferences must be uploaded by shortlisted candidatesMid-June 2026Final interview results announced9 July 2026Deadline to submit at least one unconditional UK university offerSeptember/October 2026Chevening scholars begin their studies in the UKCheck Out:How to Apply to UK Universities? | Selection: Once the applicants have submitted their scholarship application, it goes on for review. The applications are reviewed thoroughly and strictly. Thus, it is highly competitive. The process of selecting Chevening Scholars takes at least 8 months from the application deadline.Chevening scholars are selected based on a combination of strong leadership and networking potential, relevant work experience, high-quality references, and a compelling application. The different stages of review are:Initial Screening:Applications are first checked for plagiarism and reviewed to ensure they meet Chevening’s basic eligibility criteria.Reading Committee Review:Independent panels assess each application based on how well the applicant aligns with Chevening’s goals, FCDO objectives, and regional priorities.Embassy/High Commission Interview:Shortlisted candidates are interviewed by British embassies or high commissions, which make the final selection decisions.Chevening Scholarship InterviewApplicants will be notified for an interview if their application is shortlisted.The first, and most crucial thing to do upon receiving an invitation to interview is to book your interview slot.Before the interview, applicants must complete the following steps in the Chevening online application system:Upload a scanned copy of your photo ID for identity and citizenship verificationUpload your undergraduate education certificate or documentation.Upload 2 reference letters or have them submitted directly to your local Chevening team.Failure to submit all required documents by the end of the interview period will result in disqualification, regardless of interview performance.Reference GuideReference letters are a crucial part of the Chevening scholarship requirements. They must:Be written in English (translations must be notarized)Be addressed to the British embassy or high commissionInclude the referee’s contact details (address, email, and phone)Mention:How long the referee has known the applicantThe capacity in which they know the applicantDate of last regular contactAn endorsement of the applicant’s leadership, networking skills, and academic readinessReferees can either send the letters directly to the local Chevening team or return them to the applicant for uploading.

Leadership skills in different fields such as academics, community, and creative leadership are the undeniable requirements for getting Forte Fellowships for Women.Some of the core and measurable eligibility requirements for Forte scholarship program are:Student Type:Female studentsAcademic Requirements: Minimum GPA of 3.6 (or 75%) in UG studiesExam Requirements:Exam NameMinimum RequirementsIELTS6.5PTE62GMAT697 | Application: Applicants need not submit any external application form for Forte Fellowship. All the full-time MBA students who fulfill the Fellowship eligibility criteria will be automatically considered for the program.However, applicants must ensure that they provide the following documents at the time of admission to their chosen US, Canada,UK university.Letters of recommendationAcademic transcriptsStatement of purposeWork experienceUpdated resumeStandardized test scores | Selection: Applicants are automatically sorted out on the basis of their profiles and skills for the fellowship. Theiracademicandbehavioral qualitiesare kept in mind while selecting them for the scholarship.The Forte Fellowship selection process is conducted by each participating UK university.
